findings.title: Default demo persona (Anna) has Block-D steps that NEVER resolve → Umzug-Vorgang never reaches 'abgeschlossen' → inline ValueReceipt (Once-Only counter + Stammdaten source line) never renders
findings.location: src/lib/mock-backend/autopilot/umzug.ts:613-634 (Block D yields pending_eid_confirmation, generator does NOT wait); src/lib/mock-backend/api.ts:576-583 (isVorgangFullyResolved); src/components/autopilot/InlineCascade.tsx:187-203 (receipt only fetched when status==='abgeschlossen')
findings.evidence: Block D loop yields `status: 'pending_eid_confirmation'` and the comment states 'Der Generator wartet hier NICHT auf die eID-Bestätigung'. `isVorgangFullyResolved` returns true only when every step is `confirmed|failed|self_assigned` — `pending_eid_confirmation` is excluded. Anna's seed (src/data/personas.json:52 aufenthaltstitel, :123 kindergeld_bezug:true) makes BLOCK_D's Familienkasse (visibleIf kindergeld_bezug===true) and ABH (visibleIf aufenthaltstitel!==undefined) steps fire. InlineCascade.tsx:188 `if (!vorgang || vorgang.status !== 'abgeschlossen') return;`. NO component calls bestaetigeAutopilotSchritt (grep of src/components found only TermineView + ReplySheet, both unrelated), and run/page.tsx renders pending_eid_confirmation as a permanent 'In Bearbeitung' label with no confirm button (run/page.tsx:96-101).
findings.explanation: For the ONLY persona the spine/Loom uses, the headline wow #1 deliverables silently fail: the Once-Only counter ('ca. 38 Felder') and the Stammdaten source line never appear inline, and two cascade rows spin 'Wird übermittelt' forever (STATUS_LABEL_KEY maps pending_eid_confirmation→in_progress, and InlineCascade.tsx:252-254 keeps the spinner animating). The spine e2e even codifies this as expected ('The 5th card ... may remain in_progress/pending', spine.spec.ts:280-281) and never asserts the receipt renders, so it is green while the wow is broken. This is the central feature of feat/wow-1-inline-cascade.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) give InlineCascade/run-page an eID-confirm affordance that calls api.bestätigeAutopilotSchritt for each pending_eid_confirmation step (then the vorgang completes), or (b) for the inline-cascade beat, treat pending_eid_confirmation as a terminal 'angeboten/erledigt' state in a completion check so the receipt can render while still showing the eID step as citizen-confirmed-next. Add an e2e assertion that the inline Once-Only counter + source line actually render for Anna.

verdict.reasoning:
Independently reproduced from the real code on branch feat/wow-1-inline-cascade.

DATA FLOW (live inline-cascade path, the one the spine/Loom uses): AssistentView dispatches starte_umzug -> ToolCallCard renders <InlineCascade vorgangId={call.vorgangId}> (ToolCallCard.tsx:93) for the FRESH live vorgang. starte_umzug (api.ts:~1700-1751) creates a vorgang with status 'angelegt' and runs runAutopilotInBackground fire-and-forget.

GENERATOR: umzugAutopilot Block D (umzug.ts:614-634) yields steps with status 'pending_eid_confirmation' and requires_eid:true, and the inline comment confirms 'Der Generator wartet hier NICHT auf die eID-Bestätigung'.

COMPLETION GATE: runAutopilotInBackground (api.ts:~1486) only calls changeVorgangStatus(...,'abgeschlossen') when isVorgangFullyResolved(v) is true. isVorgangFullyResolved (api.ts:576-583) returns true only if EVERY step is confirmed|failed|self_assigned — 'pending_eid_confirmation' is excluded. So any surviving Block-D step blocks completion.

ANNA FIRES BLOCK D: personas.json shows Anna kindergeld_bezug:true (:123) and aufenthaltstitel defined (:52). BLOCK_D visibleIf (umzug.ts) — familienkasse: p.kindergeld_bezug===true (FIRES), abh-berlin-lea: p.aufenthaltstitel!==undefined (FIRES), kfz: p.kfz_halter===true (does NOT fire — Anna kfz_halter:false; minor auditor overstatement, immaterial). So 2 Block-D steps stall as pending_eid_confirmation.

ONLY UNSTICK PATH IS DEAD: the sole mutator that flips a Block-D step to confirmed and re-checks completion is bestaetigeImpl, exposed as api.bestaetigeAutopilotSchritt/bestätigeAutopilotSchritt (api.ts:1317-1468). Grep of all .tsx confirms NO component calls it: the only .tsx hits are decorative status-viz maps and TermineView.bestaetigeTerminVorschlag / ReplySheet 'bestaetigen' reply-mode strings — both unrelated. run/page.tsx (and VorgaengeView, VorgangDetailLoader) render pending_eid_confirmation as a permanent 'In Bearbeitung'/'current' label with no confirm affordance.

RESULT IN InlineCascade.tsx: receipt effect line 188 `if (!vorgang || vorgang.status !== 'abgeschlossen') return;` never passes -> receipt stays null -> Once-Only counter (282-286) and Stammdaten source line (288-292) never render; the 2 Block-D rows spin forever (250-254 + STATUS_LABEL_KEY pending_eid_confirmation->in_progress). Exactly the wow #1 deliverables that silently fail for the default persona.

Severity: this is the headline wow moment of the named feature branch for the only Loom persona; the receipt silently never appears and rows hang indefinitely. Critical is warranted. (Note: not a crash/throw — graceful-but-broken — but the central feature is non-functional, which the EXPLANATION correctly frames.)

findings.title: Inline-cascade Posteingang counter undercounts (often stays 0) because letters emitted before the SSE EventSource connects are dropped and never backfilled
findings.location: src/components/autopilot/InlineCascade.tsx:151-184 (counter only increments from live letter_received ticks; getVorgang backfill never seeds seenLetterIdsRef/posteingangCount); src/components/assistent/ToolCallCard.tsx:91-93 (InlineCascade mounts only after starte_umzug status==='done')
findings.evidence: InlineCascade subscribes via api.subscribe → client.ts:146-167 opens `new EventSource('/api/mock/events')` whose connection completes after an async HTTP handshake. The background cascade is fire-and-forget (api.ts:1748-1751 runWithCapturedContext(...runAutopilotInBackground)) and the first Block-A step yields synchronously at generator start before any wait. The component only mounts after startUmzug resolves (ToolCallCard.tsx:91 `isUmzugStart && call.vorgangId`). Letters are emitted via appendLetter → emit('letter_received') (api.ts:520-525); SSE has no replay, so frames sent before the EventSource is established are lost. The initial getVorgang fetch (InlineCascade.tsx:116-129) backfills steps but never populates posteingangCount or seenLetterIdsRef.
findings.explanation: wow-backlog #1 explicitly promises 'Bestätigungen sichtbar in den Posteingang' droppen. The i18n plural posteingang_landing has a `=0 {Noch keine Bestätigung im Posteingang}` branch (de.json:3005). When early letter frames are missed, the inline beat shows 'Noch keine Bestätigung im Posteingang' even though the Posteingang actually received 4+ confirmations — the opposite of the intended wow, on the exact surface the user is watching.
findings.recommendation: Seed posteingangCount/seenLetterIdsRef from the initial getVorgang snapshot (count letters already attached to the vorgang, or fetch getLetterThread(vorgangId)), and/or re-fetch the letter count on vorgang completion, so the counter reflects persisted letters independent of SSE timing rather than only live-after-connect frames.

verdict.reasoning:
The core flaw is real and reachable in the code as written. InlineCascade.tsx (lines 170-179) derives posteingangCount EXCLUSIVELY from live letter_received SSE events; neither the initial getVorgang fetch (116-129) nor the autopilot_step ticks (which carry step.letter_id) ever seed posteingangCount/seenLetterIdsRef. The app wires api = the fetch client (index.ts:14 -> client.ts), so subscribe opens new EventSource('/api/mock/events') over HTTP with an async handshake. The SSE route (events/route.ts:44) has NO replay buffer — bus.subscribe only catches events emitted after it runs — so any letter_received frame emitted before the EventSource subscribes is permanently dropped and never backfilled. The background autopilot is fire-and-forget (api.ts:1748-1751) and appendLetter emits letter_received (api.ts:520-525, runner at 1481). InlineCascade mounts only after starte_umzug resolves to done (ToolCallCard.tsx:91). The spine e2e (spine.spec.ts:246-250) asserts the inline rows are visible but never asserts the posteingang_landing count, so the bug is genuinely unguarded — the Step-6 Posteingang check reads PERSISTED letters via getLetters, which is independent of the live counter, so persisted-letter landing still works.

However the auditor materially OVERSTATES the mechanism and severity. The claim that 'the first Block-A step yields synchronously at generator start before any wait' is wrong: the synchronous first yield is an in_progress step carrying NO letter (umzug.ts:536-548); the first letter-bearing yield comes only AFTER await wait(entry.latencyMs) where the first latency is 900ms (umzug.ts:550, 113; wait=setTimeout, latency.ts:15-16). So there is a ~900ms cushion between the background run starting (and the POST returning) and the first letter emission — generous enough that the client usually mounts InlineCascade and opens the EventSource in time, catching all four letters. 'Often stays 0' is therefore not supported: a partial undercount (e.g. 3 instead of 4) under a slow EventSource handshake (cold dev route compile, network/server latency) is plausible, and a full =0 requires missing ALL letters which is a much narrower window. Net: a real, unguarded timing race on a presentation-only confirmation counter on a supporting line — it can contradict the wow-backlog 'Bestaetigungen sichtbar im Posteingang' promise — but it does not break the headline beat (rows progressing + receipt + actual persisted letters in /posteingang) and does not 'often stay 0'. That warrants medium, not high.

findings.title: InlineCascade renders permanently empty if the one-shot getVorgang fetch hits the 5% simulated error — tick handler cannot recover a null vorgang
findings.location: src/components/autopilot/InlineCascade.tsx:116-129 (single getVorgang, no retry) and 154-155 (tick handler bails when prev is null); src/lib/mock-backend/latency.ts:56-67 (5% error injection in non-reliable mode)
findings.evidence: getVorgang runs through withLatency which throws ~5% of the time when not reliable (latency.ts:62 `if (!isReliableMode() && Math.random() < errorRate) throw`). The fetch effect swallows the error ('rows simply stay empty until a tick arrives', InlineCascade.tsx:123) leaving vorgang===null. The autopilot_step tick handler then does `setVorgang((prev) => { if (!prev) return prev; ... })` (InlineCascade.tsx:154-155) — every subsequent step event is discarded because prev is null. There is no re-fetch.
findings.explanation: The deployed public demo (Vercel/GitHub link shared with stakeholders) is reached without ?reliable=1 by default; the Loom script only adds ?reliable=1 for recording (loom-script.md:6). So ~5% of live visitor sessions that trigger the Umzug will see a completely empty inline cascade for the rest of the session, despite the autopilot running and letters landing — a silent dead UI on the hero feature.
findings.recommendation: On a failed initial getVorgang, retry (the MockBackendError is retryable), or fall back to building the vorgang from the first arriving autopilot_step tick (initialize a minimal vorgang from event.vorgangId so later ticks accumulate). Do not require a successful one-shot fetch as the only path to a non-null vorgang.

verdict.reasoning:
Traced the full chain in the real code and every link holds.

1) Deployed demo wiring: src/lib/mock-backend/index.ts:14 exports `apiClient as api`, so in the browser InlineCascade's `api.getVorgang` is an HTTP POST to /api/mock and `api.subscribe` is an SSE EventSource — store + autopilot live on the server; the browser only receives ticks.

2) Error injection is real on this fetch: getVorgang (api.ts:1635) wraps its body in withLatency, which at latency.ts:65-69 throws a MockBackendError ~5% of the time when not in reliable mode. server/dispatch.ts:85 runs the method under runWithRequestContext({ reliable }), and the latency error (retryable defaulting true per errors.ts:17) maps to HTTP 503; the client reconstructs it as a retryable MockBackendError.

3) Reliable mode is off by default in the public demo: loom-script.md:6 only adds ?reliable=1 for recording. So ordinary stakeholder visitors hit the unreliable path.

4) The bug as described: InlineCascade.tsx:116-129 does a single getVorgang with deps [vorgangId] and an empty catch (no retry, no error UI) — on the ~5% error, vorgang stays null. The autopilot_step tick handler at InlineCascade.tsx:154-155 does `if (!prev) return prev;`, discarding every subsequent step; the vorgang_status_changed handler is likewise guarded (`prev ? ... : prev`), and no handler initializes vorgang from null. Effect deps never change, so there is no re-fetch for the session.

Net effect: when that one fetch fails, the cascade rows AND the value-receipt (gated on vorgang) stay empty for the rest of the session even though the autopilot is genuinely running and letters are landing — a silent dead state on the hero feature. (Minor caveat vs. the claim's wording: it is not 100% blank — the posteingang counter link still increments off `letter_received`/seenLetterIdsRef independent of vorgang, and the disclaimer renders — but the streaming cascade + receipt, i.e. the wow-moment, are dead.)

Note this confirms the failed-fetch path specifically; there is an adjacent latent race (a tick arriving before a SUCCESSFUL fetch resolves would also be dropped) which only reinforces the fragility but isn't required for this finding.

Severity medium is right: low-probability (~5%, one call), confined to one feature, fails gracefully-but-wrong (empty, not a crash), and the recommended fix (retry the retryable error, or seed a minimal vorgang from event.vorgangId on the first tick) is trivial. Not high — it neither crashes the app nor corrupts data and is self-recoverable on reload.

findings.title: Block-D Activity-Log Rechtsgrundlage for ABH cites § 86 AufenthG — the exact norm the domain spec says was deliberately removed as wrong (Strafverfolgungs-/Erhebungskanal, not address upkeep)
findings.evidence: bestaetigeImpl builds the Block-D log entry with `step.behoerde_id === 'abh-berlin-lea' ? '§ 86 AufenthG i.V.m. § 36 BMG'`. The autopilot/umzug.ts source repeatedly corrects this: line 282-283 '§87/§86 AufenthG entfernt (Strafverfolgungs-/Erhebungskanal, NICHT Adresspflege)' and the canonical log-emitter datenkategorienForEmpfaenger/emitStammdatenLogForCascadeStep uses '§ 18 PAuswG' for abh-berlin-lea (umzug.ts:466-469). The concept brief also flags eAT address is §18 PAuswG nutzergesteuert, not §86/§87.
findings.explanation: The Activity-Log / Datenschutzcockpit is exactly where a domain reviewer inspects legal basis. The two code paths for the SAME ABH transmission now disagree (§ 18 PAuswG in the cascade log vs § 86 AufenthG in the eID-confirm log), and the §86 value is the one the team explicitly identified as legally wrong for address upkeep. A GovTech/legal reviewer spotting the banned norm undermines the realism claim that the rest of the spec works hard to establish.
findings.recommendation: In bestaetigeImpl, replace the ABH branch '§ 86 AufenthG i.V.m. § 36 BMG' with the corrected '§ 18 PAuswG' (matching umzug.ts), and reconcile the kategorie (eID self-service is not a behoerde_zu_behoerde §36 BMG push). Add a ban-list grep test covering bestaetigeImpl, not just umzug.ts.

verdict.reasoning: Independently reproduced every element of the claim from the real code. api.ts:1428 (inside bestaetigeImpl, the eID-confirm path wired to the public API at lines 1813/1816) emits the Block-D Activity-Log entry for abh-berlin-lea with rechtsgrundlage '§ 86 AufenthG i.V.m. § 36 BMG'. This appendLogEntry block (lines 1437-1448) sits OUTSIDE the kfz-only guard that closes at line 1416, so it runs unconditionally for every Block-D confirm, and the ABH branch is reached. The same ABH transmission in the canonical cascade emitter returns '§ 18 PAuswG' (umzug.ts:466-469) and the BLOCK_D table entry also uses '§ 18 PAuswG' (umzug.ts:286), with an explicit code comment at 282-283 that '§87/§86 AufenthG entfernt (Strafverfolgungs-/Erhebungskanal, NICHT Adresspflege)'. The domain spec (docs/domain/umzug.md:31,44,54,66) confirms ABH address update is user-driven via a separate appointment, not an automatic §36 BMG push — so both the §86 norm AND the kategorie:'behoerde_zu_behoerde' label at line 1440 are wrong, and they are the exact values the team deliberately corrected elsewhere. The reachability is real: the abh-berlin-lea Block-D step is generated for any persona with aufenthaltstitel set (umzug.ts:291,621 — Anna, a core demo persona), so this is live demo code, not dead. The ban-list grep test (stammdaten-v1-3-ban-list-grep.test.ts) scans umzug.ts (line 39) but not api.ts, and the only test referencing §86 AufenthG (stammdaten-norm-zitate.test.ts:58) treats it as a valid aria-label glossary entry, so nothing catches this. Severity is medium not high: it is a mocked legal-basis string in the Datenschutzcockpit, not a crash/data-loss/functional failure, but it directly undermines the demo's central legal-realism claim in precisely the surface a domain reviewer inspects, and contradicts a correction the team made on purpose.

findings.title: AI tool `lese_posteingang` schema drifts from `LetterFilter` — `absender`, single-string `status`, and `max` filters silently do nothing
findings.evidence: tools.ts advertises to the model: `filter.absender` (string), `filter.status` (single enum 'ungelesen'|'gelesen'|'erledigt'), `filter.vorgang_id`, `filter.max` (integer). But `LetterFilter` (the real `api.getLetters` parameter) is: `{ unread?, vorgang_id?, aktenzeichen_query?, behoerden_kategorie?, archetype?, status?: LetterStatusFilter[], frist_innerhalb_tage?, vorgang_status? }` — it has NO `absender` and NO `max`, and `status` is an ARRAY of a different union. The dispatcher blind-casts the model's object through: `const letters = await api.getLetters(filter as Parameters<typeof api.getLetters>[0] | undefined)` (dispatch-tool.ts:101-103). `getLetters` reads `filter?.status && filter.status.length > 0` (api.ts:1565) — a string `'ungelesen'` has `.length === 9`, so it enters the branch and iterates characters, matching nothing.
findings.explanation: The tool description's most-advertised use — 'Was steht im Brief von Finanzamt? → mit absender filtern' (tools.ts:171) — cannot work: `absender` is dropped on the floor, so the model gets the FULL inbox back and must filter in-context (or hallucinate that it filtered). `status: 'ungelesen'` is treated as a 9-char status filter that matches zero letters, so 'show me my unread mail' returns an empty list. In a live Loom demo this is exactly the kind of assistant query a stakeholder will try, and it will visibly misbehave (wrong/empty results) with no error surfaced.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) translate the model's filter in `dispatchReadTool` before the call: map `absender`→a client-side name/id substring filter, wrap `status` string into `[status]`, honour `max` via `.slice`; or (b) realign the `lese_posteingang` JSONSchema to the actual `LetterFilter` shape (drop `absender`/`max`, make `status` an array). Add a zod validator + explicit mapping so the cast is no longer `as Parameters<...>` blind.

verdict.reasoning:
Independently reproduced every part of the claim from the real code.

SCHEMA DRIFT (real): tools.ts:180-199 advertises filter.absender (string), filter.status (single enum 'ungelesen'|'gelesen'|'erledigt'), filter.vorgang_id, filter.max (integer, default 10). The actual api.getLetters param LetterFilter (letter.ts:232-249) has NO absender, NO max, and status is LetterStatusFilter[] (an array over a WIDER union incl. frist buckets). So absender, max, and the status type all drift.

NO TRANSLATION / BLIND CAST (real): dispatch-tool.ts:63-69 asFilterMap only checks the value is an object and returns it verbatim; line 101-103 passes it as `filter as Parameters<typeof api.getLetters>[0]` — a pure type assertion, no zod, no key mapping. The only runtime validator imported (validateUmzugToolInput) covers the Umzug write tools, not lese_posteingang.

absender / max DROPPED (real): getLetters (api.ts:1529-1616) reads only unread/vorgang_id/archetype/aktenzeichen_query/behoerden_kategorie/frist_innerhalb_tage/status/vorgang_status. It never references absender or max (grep confirms the only absender_behoerde_id hits are unrelated field reads). So 'Brief von Finanzamt?' returns the full inbox; max never truncates.

SINGLE-STRING status MATCHES ZERO (real): api.ts:1565 `if (filter?.status && filter.status.length > 0)` — a string 'ungelesen' is truthy with length 9 > 0, so it enters; then `for (const w of wanted)` iterates the STRING's characters ('u','n','g',...), none of which equal a status or frist token, so the per-letter callback returns false for all letters → empty result. 'Show me unread mail' returns nothing, no error surfaced.

REACHABLE + PROMPT-ENCOURAGED: lese_posteingang is a registered top-level read tool dispatched at dispatch-tool.ts:99. system-prompt.ts:42 explicitly tells the model it can filter 'nach Absender, Status oder Vorgang', and :59 instructs it to call read tools proactively. tools.ts:171 names absender-filtering as the headline use. So the most natural assistant query triggers the broken paths.

Severity: high is warranted. This is the assistant — the demo's hero/spine surface — and the failure is silent (wrong/empty results, no error). The only mitigant is that for absender/max the model could re-filter in-context from the full list, but the status-string path produces a visibly empty answer. The auditor's recommendation (map absender→substring, wrap status into [status], honor max via slice, OR realign the JSONSchema + add a zod validator) is the correct fix.

findings.title: Irreversible `starte_umzug` write path skips the zod validator that exists for it; address is blind-cast
findings.evidence: `starteUmzugInput` (zod) and `validateUmzugToolInput('starte_umzug', ...)` exist and are described in tool-schemas.ts as the 'belt-and-braces' guard, but a grep for `validateUmzugToolInput` shows it is called at runtime ONLY for `preview_umzug` (dispatch-tool.ts:180) and otherwise only in `__smoke__.ts`. The actual confirm-gated write path runs `buildProposalFromStarteUmzug` which does loose manual checks — `if (!adresse || typeof adresse !== 'object' || typeof stichtag !== 'string') return null;` then `const neue_adresse = adresse as UmzugProposal['neue_adresse'];` (AssistentView.tsx:650-653) — with no `plz` 5-digit check, no `land === 'DE'`, no required `strasse`/`hausnummer`/`ort`.
findings.explanation: The single irreversible tool the demo gates the most carefully (`starte_umzug`) is the one that bypasses its own input validator. A model that emits a malformed `neue_adresse` (missing `plz`, wrong `land`, partial object) gets cast straight into `api.startUmzug`, which creates a Vorgang titled `Umzug nach undefined` and seeds the cascade with bad data. The asymmetry (preview validates, write does not) is the opposite of the intended safety posture and undermines the 'structural gate' claim in tools.ts:457-464.
findings.recommendation: In `buildProposalFromStarteUmzug` call `validateUmzugToolInput('starte_umzug', input)` and bail to `null` (or surface a confirm-card error) on `!ok`, then use the typed `data` instead of the `as UmzugProposal['neue_adresse']` cast. This also folds `block_b_consent` validation in for free.

verdict.reasoning:
Reproduced the flaw end-to-end in the real code.

REACHABILITY: When the model emits a `starte_umzug` tool_use, AssistentView `handleToolUses` (line 309) detects `requiresConfirmation('starte_umzug')` and routes to `buildProposalFromStarteUmzug(input)` (line 312). That function (AssistentView.tsx:645-664) is exactly as quoted: it does only loose manual checks — `if (!adresse || typeof adresse !== 'object' || typeof stichtag !== 'string') return null;` then `const neue_adresse = adresse as UmzugProposal['neue_adresse'];` — with NO plz/land/strasse/hausnummer/ort validation, and it never calls `validateUmzugToolInput`.

VALIDATOR EXISTS BUT IS UNUSED ON THIS PATH: `starteUmzugInput` + `neueAdresseSchema` (tool-schemas.ts:133-177) enforce plz `^\d{5}$`, `land` literal 'DE', and required strasse/hausnummer/ort, and `validateUmzugToolInput('preview_umzug'|'starte_umzug', ...)` exists (286-296). But it is invoked at runtime ONLY for `preview_umzug` (dispatch-tool.ts:180). The write path `dispatchStarteUmzug` (dispatch-tool.ts:206-234) passes `neue_adresse` straight to `api.startUmzug` with no validation. Asymmetry is real and is the inverse of the intended posture.

BAD DATA IS NOT CAUGHT DOWNSTREAM: `buildProposalFromStarteUmzug` first calls `api.previewUmzug` → `buildUmzugPreview` (autopilot/umzug.ts:755), which does NOT throw on a malformed address — it only string-interpolates `${plz} ${ort}`. So a malformed address yields a non-null proposal, the confirm card renders, and on user-confirm `api.startUmzug` (api.ts:1679) runs `titel: \`Umzug nach ${input.neue_adresse.ort}\`` → literally "Umzug nach undefined" when `ort` is missing, and seeds the cascade with undefined fields. The `Umzug nach undefined` claim is exact.

CONTRACT VIOLATION IS DOCUMENTED: tool-schemas.ts:9-10 promises the zod layer catches malformed model output, and tools.ts:457-464 claims the client enforces gating STRUCTURALLY not just by prompt. The write path defeats the data-integrity half of that stated guarantee.

ADVERSARIAL CHECKS THAT FAILED TO REFUTE: (a) The model is given a JSONSchema marking those fields required, but the whole stated purpose of the zod belt-and-braces layer is to not trust the model — so model-emitted malformed input is the explicit threat model, and it is reachable on any model error/hallucination. (b) The confirm card is not a mitigation for data integrity — it guards intent and renders from the unvalidated proposal; clicking confirm does not re-validate. (c) Not handled elsewhere.

SEVERITY: Holding at medium (not lowering). It is a genuine type-safety/contract-integrity gap on the single irreversible write tool, the asymmetry directly contradicts documented guarantees, and the fix is trivial and correct (call `validateUmzugToolInput('starte_umzug', input)`, bail/surface error on `!ok`, use the typed `data`, which also folds in `block_b_consent` validation). Not high because this is a mock/demo backend (no real records, no security/PII corruption), worst case is a cosmetically broken Vorgang plus an undefined-seeded cascade, and the trigger requires the model to violate the schema it was given.

findings.title: Posteingang tool zod validators are defined and tested but never invoked on the runtime dispatch path
findings.evidence: `POSTEINGANG_TOOL_VALIDATORS` + `validatePosteingangToolInput` are described as the layer run 'before the client-side dispatch layer executes against the mock backend' (tool-schemas.ts:6-10). But the only call sites of `validatePosteingangToolInput` are in `__smoke__.ts`. The real dispatcher for `erklaere_brief`/`extrahiere_frist`/`vorschlage_naechsten_schritt` instead uses an ad-hoc `asLetterId(input)` helper (dispatch-tool.ts:141) that just probes `input.letterId` and returns undefined otherwise. Same for `hole_ersparnis` via `asVorgangId`.
findings.explanation: Not a live-demo break (the ad-hoc extractors are functionally adequate for happy-path inputs), but it is genuine contract drift: the file-level comment claims a validation layer that the production path doesn't use, the `.strict()` rejection of extra fields never fires, and the smoke test gives false confidence that malformed tool inputs are caught before dispatch. A reviewer trusting the comment would wrongly assume inputs are zod-checked.
findings.recommendation: Either wire `validatePosteingangToolInput` into `dispatchReadTool` (replace the `asLetterId`/`asVorgangId` probes) so the documented layer actually runs, or delete the unused validators + correct the comment so the contract doc matches reality.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified against the real code. CONFIRMED with one immaterial correction.

Core claim holds: `validatePosteingangToolInput` (defined at src/lib/ai/tool-schemas.ts:114) is invoked ONLY in src/lib/ai/__smoke__.ts (grep shows call sites at lines 111,116,122,127,134,137,142,148,203,208 — all in the smoke test). The production dispatch path is AssistentView.tsx:332 → dispatchReadTool, which for erklaere_brief/extrahiere_frist/vorschlage_naechsten_schritt uses the ad-hoc asLetterId(input) helper (dispatch-tool.ts:141, defined 71-77) instead of the zod validator. So POSTEINGANG_TOOL_VALIDATORS never runs at dispatch, the .strict() extra-field rejection never fires in production, and the __smoke__.ts:148 'rejects unknown field draftAntwort' assertion gives false confidence (at runtime asLetterId would just ignore the extra field, not reject the call).

Contract drift is real and reachable: the shipped file-level comment (tool-schemas.ts:6-10) states the zod schemas 'are what we use to validate a tool_use.input before the client-side dispatch layer executes against the mock backend', and the POSTEINGANG_TOOL_VALIDATORS comment (90-96) says 'Used by the client-side dispatch path' — both false. Note the Umzug validators ARE correctly wired (validateUmzugToolInput called at dispatch-tool.ts:180), so the drift is specific to the Posteingang/ersparnis validators, which makes the inconsistency more misleading, not less.

One auditor slip, immaterial: the claim 'hole_ersparnis via asVorgangId' is wrong on two counts — asVorgangId (dispatch-tool.ts:79) backs hole_vorgang (line 121), and hole_ersparnis has NO case in dispatchReadTool at all (it would hit the default 'Unbekanntes Werkzeug' branch). This does not weaken the finding; the validator is still unused either way.

Severity: low is correct. Not a live-demo break — the model produces well-formed happy-path inputs the ad-hoc extractors handle, and the irreversible write (starte_umzug) is gated separately and DOES validate. The harm is contract-integrity/documentation: dead validators, a misleading file comment, and a smoke test that asserts a guarantee the production path doesn't provide. A reviewer trusting the comment would wrongly assume inputs are zod-checked. Reachable in code as written.

findings.title: `VorgangTyp` union is widened to `string`, defeating compile-time exhaustiveness and typo-checking on `v.typ` comparisons
findings.evidence: `export type VorgangTyp = 'umzug' | 'kindergeburt' | 'aufenthaltstitel-verlaengerung' | 'eheschliessung' | 'gewerbeanmeldung' | 'anmeldung' | string;` — the trailing `| string` collapses the whole alias to `string`. Consumers compare against literals everywhere (`v.typ === 'umzug'` in value-receipt.ts:56, dashboard/api.ts:640, VorgaengeView.tsx:150, api.ts:1458/1466/1490/1496, etc.).
findings.explanation: Because the union is really just `string`, none of the ~10 `typ === 'umzug'` / `typ === 'aufenthaltstitel-verlaengerung'` comparisons get protection against a typo (e.g. `'aufenthalstitel-verlaengerung'` would compile clean and silently never match, leaving the umzug ripple / value-receipt gating dead). It also blocks any `switch`-with-`never` exhaustiveness on Vorgang type. `VorgangStatus` by contrast is a clean closed union, so the inconsistency is asymmetric and easy to trip on. Low severity because today's literals happen to be spelled correctly, but it removes the type system's protection exactly where the autopilot gating lives.
findings.recommendation: Drop the `| string` and keep `VorgangTyp` a closed union; if open extensibility is truly needed, model it as `VorgangTyp | (string & {})` (the branded-string trick) which preserves literal autocomplete and typo-checking while still accepting arbitrary strings.

verdict.reasoning:
The claim is accurate and I reproduced it from the real code. src/types/vorgang.ts:54-61 defines `VorgangTyp` as the six literals `| string`. I confirmed via an empirical tsc --strict compile of the exact union that (a) the auditor's deliberate typo `=== 'aufenthalstitel-verlaengerung'` compiles clean with zero errors (a closed union would emit TS2367), proving typo protection is lost, and (b) `const _exhaustive: never = v` in a switch default branch errors with TS2322 "Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'never'", proving the union has collapsed to `string` and switch-with-never exhaustiveness is impossible. Both are exactly the failure modes claimed.

The cited consumers all exist as written: value-receipt.ts:56 (`vorgang.typ !== 'umzug'`, gating computeValueReceipt), dashboard/api.ts:640, VorgaengeView.tsx:150-151, and api.ts:1458/1466/1490/1496 (gating applyUmzugRipple — the umzug ripple). These sit squarely in the autopilot/value-receipt gating path, so the lost protection is in a load-bearing spot. The asymmetry claim also holds: VorgangStatus (lines 63-68) is a clean closed union with no `| string`. The type is live, not dead — re-exported via src/types/index.ts:82 and used as Vorgang.typ.

Severity is correctly low: it is a removed safety net, not a live defect. Every literal in the codebase is currently spelled correctly (e.g. 'aufenthaltstitel-verlaengerung' at VorgaengeView.tsx:356 is correct), so nothing is silently broken today; the risk materializes only on a future typo or refactor. The recommended fix (`VorgangTyp | (string & {})`) is the correct idiomatic remedy preserving open extensibility plus literal autocomplete/typo-checking.

findings.title: base-ui 1.5.0 modal backdrop uses aria-hidden, NOT inert — Tab focus escapes every modal except EudiExportDialog (keyboard-trap leak, WCAG 2.1.2)
findings.evidence: FloatingFocusManager calls `markOthers(insideElements, { ariaHidden: modal || isUntrappedTypeableCombobox, mark: false })` — `inert` is never passed. markOthers.js:72 then resolves `controlAttribute = inert ? 'inert' : ariaHidden ? 'aria-hidden' : null`, so it always sets `aria-hidden="true"` (and a CSS-only `[data-base-ui-inert]` marker at line 99), never real `inert`. aria-hidden hides from the a11y tree but does NOT remove background elements from the Tab order. The project's shared modals only run `useStripBaseUiFocusGuardAriaHidden(true)` (dialog.tsx:52, sheet.tsx:79), which only strips aria-hidden off `[data-base-ui-focus-guard]` sentinels (use-strip-base-ui-focus-guard-aria-hidden.ts:26-31) — it does nothing about the backdrop inert.
findings.explanation: Pressing Tab from inside any Dialog/Sheet/AlertDialog reaches the skip-link, header, and page content behind the dimmed backdrop while the modal is open. This is a WCAG 2.1.2 (No Keyboard Trap — here the inverse: focus leaks out) / 4.1.2 failure and a BITV 2.0 blocker. For a screen-reader/keyboard user it makes every confirmation dialog (Versand bestätigen, eID, Consent modals) unusable. The repo previously documented this and fixed exactly ONE modal (EudiExportDialog), leaving the rest leaking.
findings.recommendation: Promote base-ui's own `[data-base-ui-inert]` marker to a real `inert` attribute centrally (the pattern already proven in EudiExportDialog.tsx:78-105) inside the shared dialog.tsx/sheet.tsx `DialogContent`/`SheetContent` and in a shared hook reused by the bespoke AlertDialogs, keyed on open state, reverting on close. Also update the stale version comment in use-strip-base-ui-focus-guard-aria-hidden.ts (says 1.4.1; installed is 1.5.0).

verdict.reasoning:
The auditor's mechanical evidence is ACCURATE and I verified every cited line in the actually-installed @base-ui/react@1.5.0. FloatingFocusManager.js:322-327 calls markOthers(insideElements,{ariaHidden:modal,mark:false}) plus a separate markOthers(markerInsideElements) with defaults; markOthers.js:72 resolves controlAttribute = inert?'inert':ariaHidden?'aria-hidden':null and (line 99) sets aria-hidden="true" on the background + (line 109) only a CSS-marker data-base-ui-inert, never real inert. There is no base-ui CSS that turns that marker into focus removal. dialog.tsx:52 and sheet.tsx:79 only call useStripBaseUiFocusGuardAriaHidden(true) (which strips aria-hidden off the focus-guard SPANs, never touches inert); only EudiExportDialog.tsx:78-105 promotes the marker to real inert. The stale "1.4.1" comment in the hook is real (installed is 1.5.0). ~13 components consume the shared Dialog/Sheet without inert-promotion.

I EMPIRICALLY reproduced the leak: a one-off Playwright Tab-sweep on the shared <Sheet> (MobileNav drawer, no inert-promotion) showed realInertBody:0, and focus transiently reached the page skip-link ("Zum Hauptinhalt springen") and the header "Navigation oeffnen" button before wrapping back into the sheet. So the background-not-inert / Tab-escapes-aria-hidden mechanism is real and reachable. That substantiates the core defect.

BUT the auditor OVERSTATED it on two counts, so PARTIAL not confirmed: (1) It is NOT a permanent keyboard trap and does NOT make modals "unusable." base-ui's modal FocusGuard sentinels (FocusGuard.js: <span tabindex=0>, onFocus->enqueueFocus(first/last) when modal) and the focusout-restore handler DO re-wrap focus back into the popup within a press or two — verified empirically (focus returned into the sheet) and by two PASSING trap tests: the EUDI focus-trap test (passed live, focus only transiently hits BODY then re-enters) and the PreInsertionModal "focus-trap + ESC" Tab x6 test (an AlertDialog with NO inert-promotion, in the green suite). The leak is transient/recoverable, not a hard escape. (2) The WCAG 2.1.2 "No Keyboard Trap" citation is the wrong criterion (that rule is about being unable to LEAVE a control); the correct framing is incomplete modal focus-containment / SR-tree consistency (4.1.2, 2.4.3). The recommended fix (centralize inert-promotion in dialog.tsx/sheet.tsx + shared hook, per EudiExportDialog) is correct. Net: a genuine, reachable a11y defect across ~13 modal surfaces, but transient and self-correcting rather than a hard keyboard trap -> medium, not high.

findings.title: Inert-leak fix applied to only 1 of ~25+ modals — every shared Dialog/Sheet consumer and 11 bespoke AlertDialogs still leak
findings.evidence: Grep for `data-base-ui-inert` across src matches ONLY EudiExportDialog.tsx (the inert-promotion fix). 12 files render `AlertDialogPrimitive.Popup`; 11 leak: PreVersandModal, PreInsertionModal, ReplyDiscardConfirmDialog, ReplyTemplateSwitchConfirmDialog, SperrenAktivierenConfirmDialog, SaveConfirmDialog, PflegegradConsentModal, ReligionConsentModal, ZfdrWegweiserCard, MobilOtpMockModal, PunkteEidReauthModal. PreVersandModal.tsx:33 only calls `useStripBaseUiFocusGuardAriaHidden(open)` and its doc comment falsely claims 'shadcn-<AlertDialog> (focus-trap, aria-modal, ESC dismisses)'. Additionally 16 files render shared `<DialogContent>`/`<SheetContent>` (FilterSheet, ReplySheet, NeuerVorgangAusBriefModal, FristDetailModal, EidConfirmDialog, KorrekturwegFeBehoerdeModal, WalletMdlAttestationPreviewModal, the two TermineView dialogs at lines 974/1045, etc.) — all leak because dialog.tsx/sheet.tsx lack the inert promotion.
findings.explanation: Confirms the 'reportedly ~10 modals still leak' note is real and the count is higher (~25+ across both bespoke AlertDialogs and every shared-Dialog/Sheet consumer). Several leaking dialogs gate consequential confirm-gated actions (Versand an Behörde, eID-Bestätigung, DSGVO-Consent) — exactly the privacy-by-design moments the demo wants to showcase, so the leak undercuts the credibility pitch.
findings.recommendation: Fix once in the shared primitives (dialog.tsx, sheet.tsx) plus a shared `useInertOutsideModal(open)` hook imported by the bespoke AlertDialogs, rather than per-file. Then re-run a real Tab-sweep (not scoped [role=dialog] axe, which false-PASSes per the known base-ui note) on each modal.

verdict.reasoning: Adversarially verified and could not refute. The base-ui inert-leak is a real, documented repo bug (CLAUDE.md + MEMORY.md both note base-ui contains modal background via aria-hidden, not real `inert`, so Tab escapes the trap — WCAG 2.1.2/2.4.3). The fix (promote [data-base-ui-inert] to real `inert`) exists in exactly ONE file: EudiExportDialog.tsx:78-105. Grep for `data-base-ui-inert` across all of src matches only that file. (1) PreVersandModal.tsx:33 only calls useStripBaseUiFocusGuardAriaHidden(open); per the hook source (use-strip-base-ui-focus-guard-aria-hidden.ts:25-32) that hook only strips aria-hidden from focus-guard sentinels and does NOT add inert to background elements — so the leak is unmitigated. Its doc comment is also misleading: it claims 'shadcn-<AlertDialog> ... ESC dismisses', but the import is base-ui and base-ui AlertDialog does NOT dismiss on ESC by default (EudiExportDialog wires ESC manually at lines 140-148; PreVersandModal does not). (2) 12 files render AlertDialogPrimitive.Popup; only EudiExportDialog has the inert effect, so 11 bespoke AlertDialogs leak — all 11 named files verified present; spot-checked PunkteEidReauthModal which has zero inert handling. (3) The shared primitives dialog.tsx:52 and sheet.tsx:79 only call useStripBaseUiFocusGuardAriaHidden(true) with no inert promotion, so every DialogContent/SheetContent consumer leaks (~13 files, incl. consequential EidConfirmDialog, ReplySheet, FilterSheet, FristDetailModal, NeuerVorgangAusBriefModal, the two TermineView dialogs). Reachable: these gate Versand-an-Behörde, eID-Bestätigung, and DSGVO-consent — the privacy-by-design moments the demo showcases. Minor imprecisions only: FilterPopover is a Popover not a modal (it was not in the auditor's leak list anyway), and 2 of the 13 DialogContent/SheetContent grep hits are Rechtsgrundlage type-references rather than rendered modals — neither dents the core claim. Severity high (not critical) is correct: it is a keyboard-trap-leak a11y/BITV failure across ~22-24 modals, but a workaround pattern exists and the violation is widespread rather than a single total-blocker; the recommended fix (centralize in dialog.tsx/sheet.tsx + a shared useInertOutsideModal hook for the AlertDialogs) is sound.

findings.title: Dark mode: app chrome (logo text, sidebar nav links, header buttons) uses --brand-900 / --ink-2 which .dark never overrides → 1.05:1 and 1.4:1 contrast on every screen
findings.evidence: globals.css defines `--ink-2: #2A3656;` (:171) and `--brand-900: #0F1F4E;` (:185) as hardcoded hex in :root. The `.dark` block (globals.css:234-260) overrides only `--color-*` semantic tokens and never redefines `--ink-2` or `--brand-900`. In dark mode `.gt-header`/`.gt-sidebar` background = `var(--surface)` = `#1A1E27` (.dark). Chrome rules: `.gt-brand-logo { color: var(--brand-900) }` (prototype-v2.css:54, the 'GovTech DE' header wordmark), `.gt-sidebar-brand .crest { color: var(--brand-900) }` (:107, logo crest icon), `.gt-nav a { color: var(--ink-2) }` (:120, ALL sidebar nav link labels), `.gt-header-btn { color: var(--ink-2) }` (:69). Computed: #0F1F4E on #1A1E27 = 1.05:1; #2A3656 on #1A1E27 = 1.4:1. WCAG 1.4.3 requires 4.5:1.
findings.explanation: In dark mode the logo wordmark is essentially invisible (1.05:1) and every sidebar navigation label sits at 1.4:1 — far below the 4.5:1 AA / BITV 2.0 threshold. This is repo-wide (Topbar + Sidebar are in the (app) layout, present on all 10 screens). For a portfolio demo that explicitly claims 'WCAG 2.1 AA + BITV 2.0 mandatory', a reviewer toggling dark mode sees broken navigation immediately — a direct credibility hit. The Topbar renders `.gt-brand-logo` and Sidebar renders `.gt-nav a` via SidebarNavItem, confirming the rules apply.
findings.recommendation: Add dark overrides in the `.dark` block of globals.css for `--ink-2` (e.g. a light neutral like the existing `--color-text-primary` #ECEFF4 or secondary #B6BDC9) and `--brand-900` (a light cobalt for dark surfaces), OR re-point the chrome rules in prototype-v2.css to semantic tokens that already flip (`--color-text-primary`/`--color-text-secondary`/`--color-primary`). Verify with the axe DARK test on header/aside chrome.

verdict.reasoning: Independently reproduced the flaw from the actual code. (1) globals.css:171 defines `--ink-2: #2A3656;` and :185 defines `--brand-900: #0F1F4E;` as hardcoded hex in `:root`; grep confirms each token is defined EXACTLY ONCE in globals.css — there is no second definition. (2) The `.dark` block (globals.css:234-260) overrides only `--color-*` semantic tokens (surface, text-primary/secondary/muted, primary, success/warning/danger) and never redefines `--ink-2` or `--brand-900`, so both inherit their light :root hex in dark mode. (3) In `.dark`, `--color-surface: #1A1E27` (:235), and `.gt-header`/`.gt-sidebar` background = `var(--surface)` = `var(--color-surface)` = #1A1E27. (4) The chrome rules cited are all present in prototype-v2.css as written: `.gt-brand-logo { color: var(--brand-900) }` (:54), `.gt-sidebar-brand .crest { color: var(--brand-900) }` (:107), `.gt-nav a { color: var(--ink-2) }` (:120), `.gt-header-btn { color: var(--ink-2) }` (:69). (5) Reachability confirmed: ThemeToggle.tsx uses next-themes to cycle light→dark→system (applies the `.dark` class) and lives in the Topbar present on all (app) screens; Topbar.tsx:27 renders `.gt-brand-logo`, Sidebar.tsx:75 renders `.gt-nav` with SidebarNavItem `<a>` rows, MobileNav mirrors it. So dark mode is a real, user-triggerable feature and the rules apply on every screen. (6) Contrast math: #0F1F4E on #1A1E27 ≈ 1.04–1.05:1; #2A3656 on #1A1E27 ≈ 1.33–1.4:1 — both catastrophically below the 4.5:1 WCAG 1.4.3 / BITV 2.0 AA threshold for normal text. The finding is further corroborated by an independent prior-session memory note (project_dark_mode_chrome_contrast_bug, 2026-05-30) documenting the identical defect and noting every screen's `axe DARK` test goes RED on header/aside chrome. Severity confirmed as high (not critical): the failure is confined to app chrome rather than page content, but that chrome IS the primary navigation (logo wordmark + all sidebar nav labels + header buttons), rendered invisible/illegible on all 10 screens the moment a reviewer toggles dark mode — a direct, immediately-visible credibility hit for a demo that explicitly claims 'WCAG 2.1 AA + BITV 2.0 mandatory'. Light mode (the demo default) is unaffected.

findings.title: MonthCalendar grid becomes keyboard-unreachable after paging months via the prev/next chevrons (roving-tabindex anchor leaves the visible grid)
findings.evidence: Day buttons use `tabIndex={isActive ? 0 : -1}` where `isActive = isSameDay(day, activeDay)` (:193,:216). The prev/next chevron buttons call `onClick={() => goToMonth(addMonths(visibleMonth, ±1))}` (:152,:159); `goToMonth` only sets `visibleMonth` (:91-93) and does NOT update `activeDay`. After paging one or more full months by mouse, `activeDay` (initialised to today/selected) is no longer in the rendered 6-week window, so NO day button has `tabIndex={0}` — every cell is `-1`. The `role="grid"` container itself has no `tabIndex` (:166-172), so it is not focusable either.
findings.explanation: A keyboard user who pages to a future/past month with the chevrons can no longer Tab into the day grid at all — the entire calendar becomes keyboard-inoperable until they navigate back to the month containing today/selected. This is a WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) failure on a brand-new, uncommitted, untested component (`?? termine/MonthCalendar.tsx`), exactly the literal-port regression class the brief flagged. The roving-tabindex pattern is otherwise correct (arrow/Home/End/PageUp-Down), making this the one gap that defeats it.
findings.recommendation: When `goToMonth` changes the visible month, clamp/move `activeDay` into the new visible month (e.g. set it to the same day-of-month or the first of the new month) so exactly one day always carries `tabIndex={0}`; or make the `goToMonth` chevrons also call the `moveActive` path. Confirm with a Tab-into-grid test after paging.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified the full causal chain against the real code in C:\Users\iaiaa\govtech\src\components\termine\MonthCalendar.tsx. Day buttons use roving tabindex `tabIndex={isActive ? 0 : -1}` with `isActive = isSameDay(day, activeDay)` (lines 193, 216). The prev/next chevrons (lines 152, 159) call `goToMonth(addMonths(visibleMonth, ±1))`, and `goToMonth` (lines 91-93) ONLY calls `setVisibleMonth` — it does not touch `activeDay`. This contrasts with `moveActive` (lines 95-99), the path used by keyboard arrow/Home/End/PageUp/PageDown, which DOES update `activeDay`. So chevron paging changes the rendered 6-week window without moving the roving anchor.

I computed the window membership with date-fns for the demo's actual todayIso (2026-05-30) and several other dates: a SINGLE chevron click in either direction already removes today/activeDay from the 42-cell window. At that point no day button satisfies isActive, so every day button is tabIndex=-1. The role="grid" container (lines 166-172) has no tabIndex and the columnheader divs aren't focusable, so there is no Tab entry point into the grid — only the chevrons remain Tab-reachable. A keyboard user who pages by clicking (or any mouse-then-keyboard user) cannot Tab into the day grid; keyboard arrow nav needs an already-focused day button to start from. This is a real WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) failure.

Confirmed it's reachable and not masked: in the consumer TermineView.tsx (lines 490-496) MonthCalendar has no remount key and onSelect only fires on day-click, so chevron clicks never reset activeDay via selectedIso. Confirmed it's untested: the a11y keyboard test (tests/a11y/redesign-termine.spec.ts:156-193) exercises only arrow/Enter/PageDown (the moveActive path) and never clicks a chevron then Tabs into the grid.

Severity stays medium: the defect is recoverable (paging back to today's month, or clicking any day, restores a tabIndex=0 anchor; initial load is fine), so it's not a total app lockout, but it makes the calendar's primary control keyboard-inoperable in a common, easily-reachable state on a brand-new untested component — exactly the literal-port regression class. The auditor's fix (clamp activeDay into the new visible month in goToMonth, or route the chevrons through moveActive) is correct.

findings.title: 172-key parity gap: every non-DE locale is missing the same 172 keys (de=1976, others=1804), rendered as raw key paths
findings.evidence: Flatten+diff: de.json=1976 leaf keys; en/ru/uk/ar/tr each =1804 → exactly 172 keys missing from ALL five, 0 orphans. Concentrated in: stammdaten.mobilitaet (83), stammdaten.wallet (30), stammdaten.kontakt (26), posteingang.compose (9), stammdaten.aktivitaet (8), stammdaten.disclaimer (7), stammdaten.field_card (6), stammdaten.sektion (2), common.posteingang (1). Confirmed live-rendered: FuehrerscheinHauptkarte.tsx:70 calls t('fe_nr_label') whose full path stammdaten.mobilitaet.fe.fe_nr_label is absent in all 5; FuehrerscheinKlassenList.tsx:64-65 calls t('klassen_disclosure_expanded/collapsed') (also absent). request.ts loads a single locale (messages = (await import(`@/lib/i18n/locales/${locale}.json`)).default) with NO getMessageFallback and NO merge-onto-DE, so next-intl v3 (^3.26.3) renders the missing key path as literal text for non-DE users.
findings.explanation: The Stammdaten screen (one of the 10 routed (app) screens) renders the Mobilität/Wallet/Kontakt sub-sections. A reviewer who switches to EN/RU/UK/AR/TR sees raw dotted key strings (e.g. 'fe_nr_label', 'Fahrerlaubnis-Nummer'-slot empty) instead of translated labels across ~170 fields. For a portfolio demo whose pitch includes a 6-locale story to GovTech stakeholders, switching language on a content-rich screen and seeing debug key paths is a credibility-killer. _status.json only documents the Termine sync — this stammdaten/posteingang gap was never propagated and is undocumented.
findings.recommendation: Propagate the 172 DE-source keys to all five target locales (DE→target directly, never via EN, per existing _status.json discipline). As a safety net, also configure getMessageFallback in src/i18n/request.ts to fall back to the DE source string (or at minimum log) rather than rendering the raw key path, so future gaps degrade gracefully instead of leaking key names.

verdict.reasoning:
The auditor's STRUCTURAL facts are all independently reproduced and correct: flatten+diff gives de.json=1976 leaf keys, en/ru/uk/ar/tr each=1804, exactly 172 keys missing from all five with 0 orphans; the prefix breakdown matches exactly (mobilitaet 83, wallet 30, kontakt 26, compose 9, aktivitaet 8, disclaimer 7, field_card 6, sektion 2, common.posteingang 1); src/i18n/request.ts (verified) loads a single locale via dynamic import with NO getMessageFallback and NO merge-onto-DE; next-intl is v3.26.5 whose documented default renders the full key path on MISSING_MESSAGE; and no onError/getMessageFallback override exists anywhere. The cited components also literally call the missing keys (FuehrerscheinHauptkarte.tsx:52+70 useTranslations('stammdaten.mobilitaet.fe')+t('fe_nr_label'); FuehrerscheinKlassenList.tsx:42+64-65 the two klassen_disclosure keys), and those paths are genuinely absent in all five non-DE locales.

But the load-bearing EXPLANATION/SEVERITY claim is REFUTED on reachability. I built an import-reachability graph from all 28 app/ route entry points (page/layout/etc.): 214/363 source files are reachable. NONE of the 172 missing keys is live-rendered as a UI label in any reachable file. Specifically: the actual routed /stammdaten screen is StammdatenView.tsx — a literal HTML port that does NOT import next-intl, has NO useTranslations, imports ZERO @/components/stammdaten/* subcomponents, and never even mentions Fuehrerschein/Mobilitaet/Fahrerlaubnis. The components that DO consume the stammdaten.* keys — MobilitaetSektion (renders the two cited Fuehrerschein components), WalletSubTab, KontaktSektionsWrapper — are imported by NOTHING (orphaned, unreachable). That accounts for 162 of the 172 missing keys: not reachable on any screen, so a reviewer switching language will never see raw key paths there. So the headline 'switch to EN on the content-rich Stammdaten screen and see ~170 debug key paths' scenario does not occur in the app as written.

Of the remaining 10 non-stammdaten keys: 8 are posteingang.compose.templates.*.body_template_de — DE-source-by-design letter-body templates (the _de suffix is the repo's single-locale convention) consumed in the data layer (reply-templates.ts), not rendered as UI chrome via t(); ReplySheet IS reachable but does not reference body_template_de in render. The last two (posteingang.compose.template_loading, common.posteingang.label) are referenced by NO component at all. So even the reachable-screen surface (ReplySheet) leaks nothing.

Net: real parity gap and a real missing safety-net (no getMessageFallback) — worth fixing for the 6-locale story and to harden against future wiring of these sections — but the claimed user-visible credibility-killer is not reachable in current code. Januar 2027', and the selected-day heading in German. This is the exact 'literal-port drops localized behavior' regression class flagged in project memory (feedback_literal_port_regressions). MMMM yyyy', { locale: de })` fed into the `day_aria` t() call). findings.title: Confirm-gate for the irreversible `starte_umzug` autopilot is enforced ONLY client-side; the server RPC exposes `startUmzug` unguarded
findings.evidence: dispatch.ts builds the allowlist as `Object.keys(apiRecord).filter((key) => key !== 'subscribe' && typeof apiRecord[key] === 'function')` — so `startUmzug` (a function property of `api`) is reachable. The ONLY gate is the React orchestrator: AssistentView.tsx:309 `if (requiresConfirmation(tu.name)) { ... continue; }` plus `requiresConfirmation` in tool-schemas.ts which returns true only for `starte_umzug`. There is NO `requires_confirmation` / preview-precondition check in dispatch.ts or /api/mock/route.ts. Anyone can `POST /api/mock {"method":"startUmzug","args":[{neue_adresse,stichtag,consents,...}]}` and the cascade fires with zero confirm.
findings.explanation: The product's central, explicitly-marketed safety property is 'Nothing fires before the explicit "Umzug starten" click' and the system prompt calls it a 'HARTE REGEL'. But the gate lives entirely in client-side React state; the HTTP boundary that actually executes the write (`/api/mock`) treats `startUmzug` like any read method. For a live portfolio demo aimed at DigitalService/BMDS reviewers, the credibility claim 'confirm-gated, propose-before-act' is structurally false at the layer that matters — a GovTech-savvy reviewer opening devtools and re-POSTing the RPC bypasses the whole consent ceremony, including Block B DSGVO consent (consents array is just a caller-supplied parameter). It contradicts tools.ts:457-463 which asserts the client enforces this 'STRUCTURALLY, not just by prompt'.
findings.recommendation: Enforce the gate server-side: in dispatch.ts (or /api/mock/route.ts) treat `startUmzug` as a privileged method that requires a server-issued, single-use confirmation token minted by a preceding `previewUmzug` call for the same session+address+stichtag, OR keep an explicit server-side allowlist that excludes write methods from the generic RPC and routes them through a dedicated handler that re-checks preconditions. At minimum, document prominently that the gate is presentation-only so the demo narrative doesn't over-claim.

verdict.reasoning: Independently reproduced every link of the auditor's chain in the real code. (1) startUmzug IS a function property of the exported `api` object: api.ts:1505 `export const api = {…}` with `startUmzug:` at 1679 inside that literal; its body unconditionally writes the Vorgang, calls recordConsent(input.consents) and runAutopilotInBackground — no preview-token/precondition. (2) dispatch.ts:37-41 builds ALLOWED_METHODS as every function property of `api` except `subscribe`, so startUmzug is allowlisted and passes the only guard `!ALLOWED_METHODS.has(method)` (66). (3) /api/mock/route.ts:33-85 validates only that method is a non-empty string and args is an array, then calls dispatch(...) with no method-specific or requires_confirmation guard. (4) requiresConfirmation (tool-schemas.ts:272) is true only for starte_umzug and its sole consumer is the client React orchestrator AssistentView.tsx:309 (continue/hold-for-click); client.ts:86-100 POSTs arbitrary {method,args} to /api/mock, confirming the RPC is network-reachable with startUmzug. (5) tools.ts:457-463 explicitly claims the gate is enforced "STRUCTURALLY, not just by prompt" — contradicted by the code, since the write-executing HTTP layer treats startUmzug like any read. So a devtools re-POST of {"method":"startUmzug",...} fires the full cascade with caller-supplied DSGVO consents, bypassing the confirm ceremony. The claim is real, reachable, and correctly located. Severity downgraded from high to medium: this is a mocked, in-memory, per-session sandbox with no real authority/PII/legal effect — the "irreversible" write only mutates the attacker's own session store using consents they themselves supply, so real security/privacy blast radius is ~nil. The genuine, fixable harm is to the demo's credibility narrative (the marketed, comment-asserted "structurally enforced propose-before-act" safety property is false at the layer that matters), which warrants medium, not high.

findings.title: `/api/assistant` and `/api/dashboard/top-actions` are unauthenticated, unthrottled, and unbounded — open invitation to burn the real ANTHROPIC_API_KEY on a public demo
findings.evidence: validateBody (route.ts:240-261) only checks that `messages` is a non-empty array and persona has id/vorname/nachname — no cap on message count, no per-message length cap, no total-bytes cap. The raw `messages` array is forwarded verbatim to `anthropic.messages.stream({ ... messages })` (route.ts:146-152). There is no middleware (no src/middleware.ts), no rate limiter, no auth check. `persona.notizen` is a free-text string injected into the cached system block (system-prompt.ts:184-186) with no length bound. Each request bills a real Haiku call; SSE keeps the stream open.
findings.explanation: On a deployed Vercel demo (the stated next step), the endpoint is internet-reachable with no credential. An attacker (or a crawler) can POST oversized `messages`/`notizen` in a loop and drive Anthropic spend arbitrarily, exhaust the account, and take the headline feature offline mid-demo. Because prompt caching keys on the rendered persona/system string, attacker-controlled `notizen` also busts the cache (raising cost per call). The cost/availability blast radius lands precisely during the moment the demo is meant to impress stakeholders.
findings.recommendation: Add a Next middleware or per-route guard: (1) cap `messages.length` and total serialized body size (e.g. reject > ~32KB), cap each text block length; (2) add a lightweight rate limit keyed on the `mock_sid` cookie / IP; (3) bound `persona.notizen` length before it enters the cached block; (4) consider a soft daily token budget with graceful refusal. These are cheap and protect the live demo.

verdict.reasoning:
Every concrete factual claim reproduces against the real code as written:

1. `validateBody` (route.ts:240-261) only checks `messages` is a non-empty array and persona has id/vorname/nachname. No message-count cap, no per-message length cap, no total-bytes cap — verified by reading the function in full.
2. The raw `messages` array is forwarded verbatim to `anthropic.messages.stream({ ... messages })` (route.ts:146-152). Confirmed.
3. No `src/middleware.ts` exists (Glob returned only node_modules hits). No auth check, rate limiter, or cookie/IP guard anywhere on either route — confirmed by reading both routes and a structural grep for rateLimit/sizeLimit/content-length/mock_sid across src; the only MAX_ constants found are unrelated Posteingang file-upload limits (letter.ts, ReplySheet.tsx), and `MAX_TOOL_ROUNDS=4` is a client-side loop bound that a direct POST attacker bypasses.
4. `persona.notizen` is injected unbounded into the cached system block (system-prompt.ts:184-186). Confirmed verbatim.
5. `/api/dashboard/top-actions` validates only `Array.isArray(body.candidates)` (route.ts:50) and forwards to a real billed AI call (prioritizeTopActionsAi). Confirmed.
6. `getAnthropicClient` reads a real `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from env and makes a real Haiku 4.5 call (client.ts:33-44). Confirmed.

The path is reachable on the stated next step (public Vercel deploy). README explicitly states a valid key IS required for the headline cascade/Wow demo (README:172), so the "key is optional / degrades gracefully" mitigation does not apply to the demo scenario the attack targets — to run the hero feature you must deploy a live key, and at that point an unauthenticated attacker can loop oversized POSTs to burn spend and/or knock the headline feature offline. Minor existing mitigations (detectRefusal short-circuit for a few categories; key optionality) do not block a benign-looking flood.

Corrected severity = medium rather than the claimed high: this is a mocked portfolio demo with no real PII and no real money beyond one API key — all citizen data is synthetic and lives client-side; the route only forwards client-supplied persona summaries, so there is no data-breach or privilege-escalation dimension. Blast radius is bounded to API spend (which Anthropic account spend-caps can backstop) and availability of a demo feature. The flaw is real, trivially exploitable, lands on the single most important demo moment, and the recommended guards (body-size/message-count/notizen caps + lightweight cookie/IP rate limit) are cheap — so it warrants fixing before public deploy, but the impact ceiling sits below a true security/privacy 'high'.

findings.title: `.gitignore` ignores `.env*.local` but not a bare `.env`, so a key placed in `.env` would be committable
findings.evidence: `.gitignore` env lines are `.env*.local` and `.env.local` only — a plain `.env` file is NOT ignored. `.env.example:4` documents `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=` and client.ts:39 instructs 'Lege ihn in .env.local an', but Next.js also auto-loads `.env`. A contributor who creates `.env` (a common habit) with a real key would have it tracked by git. Confirmed no secret is currently committed (`git ls-files` shows only `.env.example`; no `sk-ant` literal in tree).
findings.explanation: Low because the documented path is `.env.local` (which is ignored) and nothing is leaked today. But for a repo explicitly meant to be published on GitHub as a portfolio piece, one wrong filename leaks a live billing credential. The footgun is one typo away.
findings.recommendation: Broaden the ignore to cover `.env` and all variants, e.g. add `.env` and `.env*` (keeping a `!.env.example` negation) to `.gitignore`.

verdict.reasoning: Reproduced the flaw directly from the real files. .gitignore (C:\Users\iaiaa\govtech\.gitignore) lines 6-7 are exactly `.env*.local` and `.env.local` — neither matches a bare `.env`. Confirmed with git: `git check-ignore .env` returns exit 1 (NOT ignored); `.env.local` is matched (`.gitignore:7`); `.env.production` is also NOT ignored. Next.js is `^15.1.4` (package.json), which per documented behavior auto-loads `.env` in addition to `.env.local`, so a key placed in `.env` would be both loaded and git-trackable. client.ts:39 indeed instructs `.env.local` (the ignored, safe path), and .env.example documents `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=` (on line 3, not line 4 as cited — a trivial off-by-one in the cite, no substantive impact). `git ls-files` shows only `.env.example` tracked, so nothing is leaked today. The claim is real and reachable: the leak requires a contributor to deviate from the documented `.env.local` to a bare `.env` AND commit it, so it is a latent footgun rather than an active vuln — 'low' is the correct severity, matching the auditor. The recommendation to add `.env`/`.env*` with a `!.env.example` negation is sound.

`, section titles (Letzte Aktivitäten, Einwilligungen), button labels (Verwalten, Ein/Aus, Datenexport, Einstellungen), dialog titles (Zugriffsprotokoll, Einwilligungshistorie) all hardcoded; useTranslations used only for dynamic zweck_i18n_key log lookups. - FamilieView.tsx: line 383 `Haushalt verwalten` verified verbatim; header/household card/rail/badges hardcoded (the vorgaenge/nachweise lists DO use t('familie...'), so the file is partial — consistent with the claim). - SteuerView.tsx: line 260 `title="In dieser Demo nicht aktiv"` verified verbatim; entire static UI (Steuer, Entwurf, Ihr Fortschritt, Übersicht der Steuerbereiche, badge labels, Wichtige Fristen, Verwendete Nachweise) hardcoded. Reachability is confirmed and material: LanguageSwitcher.tsx is real, exposes all 6 locales (de/en/ru/uk/ar/tr), writes a locale cookie, and is mounted in Topbar.tsx which lives in the shared (app)/layout.tsx — so it renders on all four screens. Switching to a non-DE locale on /dokumente, /datenschutz, /familie, /steuer leaves the bulk of each screen in German. The commit message a3be2bb confirms the auditor's quote verbatim ("i18n followup: new strings hardcoded German (these are prototype-port surfaces)"). Aggravating: the en.json and de.json locale files ALREADY contain the matching keys (dokumente.title, dokumente.search.placeholder "Suche nach Dokumenten", dokumente.action.ansehen, etc.) — the literal-HTML port orphaned them, so these screens regressed from i18n-complete to hardcoded German while the translations sit unused in all 6 locales. Remediation is trivial (re-wire t() to existing keys). This violates the CLAUDE.md hard rule "Strings: never hardcoded. Always via t('key.path')" and contradicts the baseline claim "6-locale i18n at parity." But the flaw is unquestionably real, reachable via a live demoed control, and a credibility hit if a stakeholder toggles the language during the Loom — so it is not a refutable nit. findings.title: Document preview + download expose raw Behörde ID instead of the authority name

findings.explanation: The 'Ansehen' preview is one of the controls this branch's sweep just wired (a3be2bb). A citizen opening a document preview sees a developer slug (e.g. `berlin-ea-mitte`) in the user-facing 'Ausstellende Behörde' row instead of 'Bürgeramt Mitte'. CLAUDE.md requires mock PII to 'look real'; leaking an ID into the one field most likely to be screenshotted undercuts the realism the whole artefact is selling. The control technically works, but its output is wrong.
findings.recommendation: Load `api.getBehoerden()` (already used elsewhere in this view's ecosystem) into a `behoerdenById` map and resolve `behoerdenById[doc.ausstellende_behoerde_id]?.name_de ?? doc.ausstellende_behoerde_id` in both the preview dialog row and the download text.

verdict.reasoning: Every factual claim in the finding is verified against the real code. C:\Users\iaiaa\govtech\src\components\dokumente\DokumenteView.tsx line 837 renders `<dd>{doc.ausstellende_behoerde_id}</dd>` under the `<dt>Ausstellende Behörde</dt>` label in the preview dialog, and line 263 writes `Ausstellende Behörde: ${doc.ausstellende_behoerde_id}` in handleDownloadDoc. The field is typed `BehoerdeId` (src/types/document.ts:40) — an internal slug — and the Document type has no behoerde-name field. A Grep over the whole component confirms it performs NO name lookup (no behoerden/getBehoerden/name_de references). The proper display names exist in behoerden.json (e.g. buergeramt-berlin-mitte -> "Bezirksamt Mitte von Berlin — Bürgeramt Müllerstraße") and api.getBehoerden() exists (api.ts:790,1515), so the recommended fix is feasible. Both controls are wired and reachable: the "Ansehen" button (line 456) sets previewDoc, the "Herunterladen" button (line 464) calls handleDownloadDoc; the preview dialog receives previewDoc (line 686). So a citizen does see a raw slug in a user-facing, screenshot-likely field, undercutting the "look real" realism mandate in CLAUDE.md. I could not refute any element.
