That is a realism-polish defect, best rated low.

findings.title: Termine ICS button keeps singular per-Termin aria-label but now exports ALL future termine
findings.evidence: `handleIcsExport()` iterates `futureTermine.forEach(...)` into one `MOCK-termine.ics` blob (lines 417-440), yet the button's accessible name is `t('termine.card.ics_aria', { betreff: naechster.betreff })` = "Termin {betreff} als ICS exportieren" (singular, named after only the next Termin) and the visible label is `t('termine.card.ics')` = "ICS exportieren". The deleted `buildIcs.ts` was per-Termin (`${termin.id}.ics`); the spec (docs/specs/redesign-termine.md:103) describes a per-Termin `buildIcs(termin, behoerde)`.
findings.explanation: Not dead — the export works and is valid VCALENDAR with `[MOCK]`. But the control's name lies about its scope: a screen-reader user is told they are exporting the single named appointment while the file actually contains every upcoming appointment. This is the inverse of the prior status-filter no-op bug (the control does MORE than it says), and a minor WCAG 2.4.6 (label describes purpose) mismatch.
findings.recommendation: Either make the aria-label/label plural (e.g. 'Alle anstehenden Termine als ICS exportieren') or restore the per-Termin export so the singular label is accurate.

verdict.reasoning: Reproduced directly from the code as written. In src/components/termine/TermineView.tsx, handleIcsExport() (line 403) iterates futureTermine.forEach(...) (line 417) and emits ONE multi-VEVENT blob downloaded as 'MOCK-termine.ics' (line 440). futureTermine (line 290) = visibleTermine (all termine passing the active filter, line 266) reduced to those from ~now onward — i.e. genuinely every upcoming appointment when no behörde filter is set, not just one. The button (lines 821-835) sets its accessible name via aria-label = t('termine.card.ics_aria', { betreff: naechster.betreff }), and naechster is the single next appointment (dayTermine[0], line 307). de.json line 2578 confirms ics_aria = 'Termin {betreff} als ICS exportieren' — singular, named after only the next Termin. So the accessible name announces export of one specifically-named appointment while the file contains all future appointments. The mechanism, the lines, and the i18n strings all match the auditor's evidence; the control is reachable and the mismatch is real. Two minor refinements to the auditor's framing: (1) the VISIBLE label t('termine.card.ics') = 'ICS exportieren' is scope-neutral and not itself inaccurate — only the aria-label lies about scope, affecting screen-reader users specifically; (2) this is a WCAG 2.4.6/name-accuracy nit, not a broken or dead control — the export is functional and produces valid VCALENDAR with [MOCK], and the divergence is benign (SR users receive MORE than announced, not a failed action). Severity 'low' is correct: cosmetic accessible-name inaccuracy, no functional breakage, supporting-track surface.

findings.title: Steuer/Dokumente honestly-inert buttons use disabled — keyboard users can't reach the explanatory tooltip
findings.location: src/components/steuer/SteuerView.tsx (bereich-action button, disabled+title) and src/components/dokumente/DokumenteView.tsx:478-486 (Mehr button, disabled+title)
findings.evidence: Steuer bereich-action: `<button ... disabled aria-disabled="true" title="In dieser Demo nicht aktiv" ...>`. Dokumente: `<button ... aria-label="Mehr" title="Weitere Aktionen (in Planung)" disabled aria-disabled="true">`. `disabled` removes the element from the tab order, so the `title` (the only place the 'why' lives) is mouse-hover-only.
findings.explanation: The sweep's stated principle is 'every control does what it implies, or isn't styled interactive.' Making these honestly-inert is the right call, but a native `disabled` control is unreachable by keyboard, so a keyboard/AT user gets a greyed control with no announced reason — they can't discover it's 'in Planung'. The hardcoded German `title` (see finding 1) compounds this for non-DE users.
findings.recommendation: For honestly-inert affordances, prefer a non-disabled element with `aria-disabled="true"` plus visible (not title-only) helper text, or remove the control entirely as Steuer did with the dead MoreVertical button. Localize the explanatory text.

verdict.reasoning: Both cited locations reproduce exactly as written. Steuer (src/components/steuer/SteuerView.tsx:255-264): a <button> showing an action label with `disabled aria-disabled="true" title="In dieser Demo nicht aktiv"` and cursor:not-allowed. Dokumente (src/components/dokumente/DokumenteView.tsx:478-486): <button aria-label="Mehr" title="Weitere Aktionen (in Planung)" disabled aria-disabled="true"> with a MoreVertical icon. The technical claim is correct: native `disabled` controls are removed from the tab order and are not focusable, and a `title` tooltip is mouse-hover-only (and generally not announced by screen readers), so the only carrier of the "why this is inert" rationale is unreachable to keyboard/AT users. I checked the surrounding markup in both files — there is no adjacent visible helper text conveying the demo-inert rationale through another channel (the Steuer status badge says Geprüft/Ergänzen/Nicht vorhanden, unrelated to the control being inert). Both title strings are hardcoded German, not run through next-intl t(), confirming the i18n compounding noted in the related finding. Severity stays low: these are deliberately, honestly-inert affordances (no false dead control), the defect is a discoverability/a11y gap, and both screens are supporting surfaces, not the demo spine. The recommendation (focusable element with aria-disabled + visible localized helper text, or removal as Steuer already did for its other dead button) is appropriate.

findings.title: Headline wow miscounts the actors as "six Behörden" — two of the six are not Behörden, contradicting the project's own non-negotiable rule
findings.evidence: Headline wow: "...notifies every competent Behörde for them — Einwohnermeldeamt, Finanzamt, KFZ-Stelle, Krankenkasse, Rundfunkbeitrag, Arbeitgeber" (demo-spine.md:11). Loom narration: "Einwohnermeldeamt, Finanzamt, KFZ-Stelle, Krankenkasse, Rundfunkbeitrag, Arbeitgeber. Sechs Stellen..." (loom-script.md:19) and "Sechs Meldungen, ein Satz" (loom-script.md:83). The project's own guardrail flatly forbids this: "Beitragsservice (Anstalt) + Arbeitgeber (privat) sind **KEINE Behörden** und zählen nicht in einen 'X Behörden'-Count" (wow-backlog.md:21).
findings.explanation: Of the six named actors, the Beitragsservice ARD/ZDF/Dlr is a non-rechtsfähige Gemeinschaftseinrichtung of the Landesrundfunkanstalten (not a Behörde), and the Arbeitgeber is a private employer (not a Behörde). The Krankenkasse is a Körperschaft d.ö.R. in Selbstverwaltung, not a classic Behörde either. A DigitalService/BMDS reviewer hears "sechs Behörden" and immediately knows at least two are wrong — this is the single most repeated spoken line in the Loom (the memorable "Sechs Meldungen, ein Satz"), so the error is front-and-center in the artefact's headline moment. It also makes the demo internally inconsistent: the honest value-receipt code excludes exactly these actors from behoerden_count (value-receipt.ts:32-40), so the spoken 'six Behörden' will not match the number the UI computes.
findings.recommendation: Stop calling it 'sechs Behörden'. Reframe the headline/Loom line to a count-neutral or actor-accurate phrasing, e.g. 'sechs Stellen' / 'sechs Empfänger' / 'jede zuständige Stelle' — or split it honestly: 'vier öffentliche Stellen automatisch nach dem Meldegesetz, dazu Versicherer/Arbeitgeber mit Ihrer Einwilligung.' Keep the spoken count consistent with the value-receipt's behoerden_count. The word 'Behörde' must only cover Bürgeramt, Finanzamt, KFZ-Stelle, and (with the GKV caveat) the Krankenkasse.

verdict.reasoning:
The auditor's German-legal facts are correct (verified by web search + the project's own domain doc): the Beitragsservice is a "nicht rechtsfähige Verwaltungsgemeinschaft" of the Rundfunkanstalten and explicitly "weder eine Behörde"; the Arbeitgeber is private; both are excluded from any "X Behörden" count by the project's own rule (wow-backlog.md:21, spec C5, and value-receipt.ts:28-40 `PRIVATE_ODER_ANSTALT`). And demo-spine.md:11 does loosely write "every competent Behörde" over a six-item list that includes Rundfunkbeitrag + Arbeitgeber. To that extent the critique has a real, narrow basis.

But the critique's load-bearing claims are refuted by the actual files:

1. The Loom does NOT say "sechs Behörden." The auditor's own cited lines say "Sechs Stellen" (loom-script.md:19) and "Sechs Meldungen, ein Satz" (lines 83, 125). The "single most repeated spoken line" the auditor flags as the error — "Sechs Meldungen, ein Satz" — is actor-accurate: six Meldungen/transmissions did go out, and "Meldung"/"Stelle" are count-neutral regardless of recipient legal type. The auditor conflated a non-existent "sechs Behörden" with the actually-spoken "sechs Meldungen/Stellen." The headline moment is therefore NOT wrong.

2. The internal-inconsistency claim is backwards. The one UI string that pairs a number with the word "Behörden" (de.json:364 triumph.subtitle, "Umzug an {count} Behörden gemeldet") is fed by behoerden_count itself — word and number are consistent by construction. Every UI string that counts the FULL fan-out deliberately says "Stellen" (de.json:583 hero "{count} Stellen", 652 "Beteiligte Stellen ({count})"), and the inbox even labels recipients "Privatrechtl. (behördenartig)" (833). The codebase has ALREADY implemented the auditor's exact recommendation; nothing in the running product speaks "six Behörden" against a different computed number.

3. value-receipt.ts confirms the live count for Anna's Block-A run excludes Beitragsservice + Bundesdruckerei (and Block-B Arbeitgeber/bank/etc.), so the UI's "Behörden" number is honest. The domain source-of-truth §1a is meticulous: UI text is "bis zu 8 Stellen" (full fan-out) vs "6 Behörden" only as Anna's internal Loom-reference label.

Net: the viewer-facing artefacts (Loom narration + running UI/i18n + count code) are already correct and already do what the auditor recommends. The only genuine residue is one loosely-worded English headline sentence in an INTERNAL orchestration doc (demo-spine.md:11), not in the Loom, UI, or the headline moment a stakeholder actually sees. That is a one-line copy-polish, not a critical credibility defect. Severity downgraded critical -> low. Verdict partial: legal facts sound + one trivial internal-doc wording slip true, but the central "headline/Loom miscounts six Behörden and contradicts the UI" thrust is a strawman against text that doesn't exist.

findings.title: Loom claims Krankenkasse, KFZ and Arbeitgeber are notified automatically by saying one sentence — but none of these is push-fed from the Melderegister
findings.evidence: "Eine Behörde nach der anderen empfängt die Adressänderung: Einwohnermeldeamt, Finanzamt, KFZ-Stelle, Krankenkasse, Rundfunkbeitrag, Arbeitgeber. Sechs Meldungen, ein Satz." (loom-script.md:83). The domain doc contradicts this for three of the six: KFZ needs an active §15 FZV notice by the Halter ("Kein §36-BMG-Push aus dem Melderegister — der Halter muss aktiv mitteilen", umzug.md:27); Krankenkasse "ist nicht im Melderegister" / needs Einwilligung (umzug.md:30,80); Arbeitgeber is purely privatrechtlich and needs consent (umzug.md:35).
findings.explanation: The narration flattens four legally distinct mechanisms (Block A automatic §36-BMG push; Block B consent-gated DSGVO; Block D eID-confirmed pre-fill) into one undifferentiated 'sechs Meldungen, ein Satz' sweep. The actual UI is correct and granular (the confirm card groups by block, Scene 4 even says so), but the spoken Scene-5 line undoes that nuance and asserts the legally-impossible version: that one sentence auto-notifies the KK, the KFZ-Stelle and the employer. That is exactly the '"eine Adresse, viele Stellen"-Trugschluss' the domain doc calls out as the central citizen misconception (umzug.md:64). An insider will catch the contradiction between Scene 4 (honest) and Scene 5 (overclaim).
findings.recommendation: Rewrite the Scene-5 narration to preserve the block distinction the UI already shows: 'Vier Stellen automatisch nach dem Meldegesetz; Krankenkasse, Bank und Arbeitgeber nur mit meiner Einwilligung; KFZ-Stelle und Familienkasse erst, nachdem ich mit dem Ausweis bestätigt habe.' Keep 'ein Satz' as the trigger framing, but never imply 'one sentence = six automatic pushes.'

verdict.reasoning:
GROUNDED CORE: Both cited lines verified verbatim (loom-script.md:83 Scene 5; :125 Cold-Open). The legal premise checks out: domain doc (umzug.md:27,30,35,64,79-81) and independent web research both confirm the Meldeamt does NOT push-feed Krankenkasse, KFZ-Zulassungsstelle, or Arbeitgeber — the citizen must notify each (KFZ via active §15 FZV notice, KK/employer separately). So a spoken line that lists six recipients and concludes "Sechs Meldungen, ein Satz" with no audible qualifier does flatten the four-block, three-legal-basis distinction that Scene 4 (line 71) carefully draws, and risks reinforcing the exact "eine Adresse, viele Stellen"-Trugschluss the domain doc names (umzug.md:64). It also mislabels Beitragsservice (Anstalt) and Arbeitgeber (private) as "Behörden" ("Eine Behörde nach der anderen"), which the brief explicitly forbids in a tally. The recommendation to restore the block distinction in narration is sound and matches the project's own guardrails. This is a real, fixable narration-tightness defect.

WHERE THE AUDITOR OVERREACHES (why partial, not confirmed, and severity down to medium): (1) The line does NOT actually assert the "legally-impossible version" the auditor claims — it never says "automatisch aus dem Melderegister" for the six. In the demo's explicit 2027 speculative fiction, consent (Scene 3: "Krankenkasse und Bank ja") and eID are gathered BEFORE the single "Umzug starten" click, so the autopilot sending all six after that is the speculative premise of the whole product, not a false claim about today's law. "Sechs Meldungen, ein Satz" is defensible within that frame. (2) The contradiction is bracketed: Scene 4 is honest and granular, and Scene 7 carries a mandatory speculative/[MOCK] disclaimer. (3) The project's OWN canonical headline-wow (demo-spine.md:11) uses the identical six-item list — so Scene 5 is consistent with the product thesis, not a rogue overclaim. The weakness is nuance-erosion in the spoken track of the hero beat, not a high-severity legal falsehood. Strategic impact: a domain insider could note Scene 4↔5 tension, so tightening the narration is worth doing — hence medium, not high or none.

findings.title: Finanzamt is labelled a Land authority in the cascade, but the spine/Loom 'Behörden' tally and consent framing never surface the federalism layer the wow-backlog mandates
findings.evidence: Guardrail: 'Föderalismus präzise: ... Familienkasse/antragsloses Kindergeld = Bund; Finanzamt = Land; Standesamt/Bürgeramt/Kfz/Kita = kommunal' (wow-backlog.md:21). The domain table assigns Finanzamt 'Land', KFZ 'kommunal', Familienkasse 'Bund', Bürgeramt 'kommunal (Vollzug Land)' (umzug.md:23-29). The Loom enumerates them as one flat list of 'Behörden' with no federal-level distinction (loom-script.md:83).
findings.explanation: This is a missed-credibility item rather than a wrong claim: the data model carries the correct foederale_ebene per Behörde, but the headline narration presents the Meldeamt, Finanzamt and KFZ-Stelle as an undifferentiated row. For the target audience (DigitalService/BMDS, who live and breathe the Bund/Land/Kommune split and the fact that the Meldewesen is kommunal-im-Auftrag-des-Landes), making the federalism legible is precisely the detail that signals insider competence. Leaving it implicit is a soft miss, not a falsehood — hence low.
findings.recommendation: Either let the inline-cascade row or the value-receipt surface the federal level per actor (the field exists), or add one Loom sentence: 'Meldeamt und KFZ-Stelle kommunal, Finanzamt Land, Familienkasse Bund — und genau diese Koordination über die Ebenen hinweg nimmt das System der Bürgerin ab.' This turns a latent weakness into a competence signal.

verdict.reasoning:
The auditor's FACTS check out but its FRAMING is overstated, so this is partial, not confirmed.

WHAT'S TRUE (verified):
- Finanzamt = Landesbehörde is correct (BMF: "Die Finanzämter sind örtliche Landesbehörden", FVG). The umzug.md table (line 26) and specs/umzug.md:423 both label it `land` correctly.
- The data model carries the correct foederale_ebene per Behörde (umzug.md:21-33 "Föderale Ebene" column; specs/umzug.md:422-428).
- The Loom (loom-script.md:19,83,125) does present Meldeamt/Finanzamt/KFZ as a flat "sechs Behörden / sechs Meldungen" list with no Bund/Land/Kommune distinction. Confirmed.
- The auditor explicitly concedes "missed-credibility item rather than a wrong claim" — i.e. no falsehood is alleged. German federalism facts (Meldewesen kommunal but BMG = Bundesrecht post-Föderalismusreform I 2006; Finanzamt = Land) are accurate.

WHY IT'S NOT CONFIRMED (strawman element):
- The TITLE asserts the federalism layer is something "the wow-backlog mandates." This is false. wow-backlog.md:21 is a NEGATIVE precision-guardrail under "Querschnitts-Realismus-Disziplin" — its operative payload is the anti-overclaim rule "Beitragsservice (Anstalt) + Arbeitgeber (privat) sind KEINE Behörden und zählen nicht in einen X-Behörden-Count." It says "get the federal level right IF you state it," not "you must SURFACE the federal level in the Loom/UI." The critique manufactures a mandate the doc doesn't contain.
- wow-backlog #1's actual mandated UI additions (lines 37, 50) are: per-Behörde consequence receipt, Once-Only counter, Stammdaten source line, Posteingang landing. foederale_ebene is deliberately NOT among them.
- It attacks an intentional Loom scoping decision. demo-spine.md:9-13 fixes the Loom to ONE headline; loom-script.md:137 explicitly says "Einmal sagen, nie wiederholen" — don't layer extra taxonomy onto a 3-min narration. The federalism split is correctly carried where it belongs (domain doc + data model), not the hero narration.

STRATEGIC IMPACT: negligible. No false statement, audience is not misled. The recommendation (surface federal level per actor) is a legitimate cheap, on-brand POLISH/competence-signal — worth doing — but its absence is not a credibility risk. Severity stays low (real, harmless enhancement; not none because the field genuinely exists and surfacing it is cheap).

NOTE — the auditor missed the sharper adjacent issue: the same Loom tally counts Rundfunkbeitrag (Anstalt) and Arbeitgeber (privat) as "Behörden" (loom-script.md:43 "sechs Behörden abzuklappern", :125 "jede zuständige Behörde"), which directly violates the project's OWN guardrail (wow-backlog.md:21; concept brief). That is a real overclaim the federalism critique did not raise — but it is outside this filed item's scope.

findings.title: The verifier-mandated loss-of-control mitigation (Block D "Sie bestätigen mit eID") has NO confirmation affordance in the demo — eID steps spin forever labelled "Wird übermittelt", reading as data-transmission-without-consent
findings.evidence: Spec §331 mandates Block D: "Bürger:in tippt eID an (1.5 s Pulse-Animation), dann läuft Bestätigung wie Block A" and the wow-backlog flag (c) says eAT-address is "§18 PAuswG nutzergesteuert, NICHT auto-gepusht". But the generator yields Block D at status:'pending_eid_confirmation' and comments "Der Generator wartet hier NICHT auf die eID-Bestätigung — die UI-Schicht ruft api.bestätigeAutopilotSchritt(...) auf" (umzug.ts:631-633). Grep across all of src/ shows bestätigeAutopilotSchritt/bestaetigeAutopilotSchritt has ZERO callers in any component — neither InlineCascade nor run/page.tsx ever invokes it. InlineCascade maps both needs_eid and pending_eid_confirmation onto STATUS_LABEL_KEY 'in_progress' → row_status.in_progress = "Wird übermittelt" (de.json) with a spinning Fingerprint icon (STATUS_VIZ lines 49-50, 75-76, 253-254).
findings.explanation: Block D is the precise feature the concept-verifier added to answer the loss-of-control objection: KFZ-Stelle, Familienkasse and Ausländerbehörde must NOT be auto-pushed, the citizen must actively tap eID. In the running demo that consent gate does not exist. Worse, a privacy-sensitive viewer sees rows for the Familienkasse and Ausländerbehörde sitting at "Wird übermittelt" (= "being transmitted") with no tap, which communicates the exact opposite of the promise: the system appears to transmit identity-card-bound data to authorities without the eID confirmation it claims to require. A CCC/Datenschützer reviewer watching the cascade would conclude the 'confirm-gate' is theatre — the one explicit-consent moment for the most sensitive recipients is missing and silently elided into an auto-progressing spinner.
findings.recommendation: Implement the eID-tap affordance the spec mandates: render needs_eid/pending_eid_confirmation rows with a distinct "Mit eID bestätigen" button (and its own status label, NOT "Wird übermittelt") that calls api.bestätigeAutopilotSchritt(vorgangId, stepId); show the 1.5s pulse, then transition to confirmed. At minimum, give the eID-pending state its own honest label (e.g. "Wartet auf Ihre eID-Bestätigung") so it never reads as transmission-in-progress.

verdict.reasoning:
Every factual claim verifies in the code. (1) InlineCascade.tsx:75-76 collapses both needs_eid and pending_eid_confirmation onto STATUS_LABEL_KEY 'in_progress' → row_status.in_progress = "Wird übermittelt" (de.json:3009), with the Fingerprint icon spinning (lines 50, 254). (2) umzug.ts:613-634 yields Block D at status:'pending_eid_confirmation' with an explicit comment that the generator does NOT wait — the UI must call api.bestätigeAutopilotSchritt. (3) That confirm function has ZERO callers anywhere: it exists only as an api.ts definition + client/dispatch plumbing + the umzug.ts comment. Neither InlineCascade nor run/page.tsx ever invokes it, so the eID consent gate can never be closed in the running demo.

The defect is actually broader than the auditor stated: the spec-mandated eID-tap affordance (§331) is wired NOWHERE. The honest label and dialog strings exist (run.status.needs_eid = "eID-Tap erforderlich", eid_dialog.cta_confirm = "Mit eID bestätigen", de.json:617-623) but no component renders a tap button or dialog; even the older run/page.tsx has no eID UI. Block D steps sit permanently at pending_eid_confirmation.

Severity grounding: the worst case is the DEFAULT demo path, not hypothetical. DEFAULT_PERSONA_ID = 'anna-petrov' (seed.ts:72), and Anna has both aufenthaltstitel (personas.json:52) and kindergeld_bezug:true (line 123). Block D visibleIf gates (umzug.ts:253/271/291) therefore fire the Familienkasse and Ausländerbehörde rows — exactly the two most privacy-sensitive recipients the concept-verifier said must NOT be auto-pushed — and the assistant mounts InlineCascade directly under starte_umzug (ToolCallCard.tsx:93), which is the headline-wow Loom path. So during the actual demo, a Datenschützer/CCC viewer sees Familienkasse and Ausländerbehörde stuck at "Wird übermittelt" with a spinner and no tap, reading as eID-bound transmission without the consent the demo claims to require — the precise inversion of the loss-of-control mitigation. The critique is specific, non-strawman, grounded in the project's own four-block model and in real German law (§18 PAuswG eID is genuinely user-controlled; eAT/KFZ/Familienkasse genuinely not auto-pushed), and the recommendation is correctly scoped.

Downgraded critical→high: no real data moves (all mocked), nothing crashes, and the headline "sechs Meldungen, ein Satz" Block-A beat still plays; the harm is a misleading label + missing affordance on the most sensitive sub-moment, not a claim-falsifying or spine-breaking failure. That is a serious demo-facing trust defect on the hero path for the exact stakeholders being courted — comfortably high, short of critical.

findings.title: For the demo persona (Anna), the autopilot never completes — so the Value-Receipt, Once-Only counter and Stammdaten-source line (the whole "hier ist der Beleg" payoff of wow-#1) never render

Anna's persona flags (personas.json) are kindergeld_bezug=true and aufenthaltstitel present → BLOCK_D fires Familienkasse + Ausländerbehörde, both yielded at pending_eid_confirmation and never confirmed (see finding above). InlineCascade gates the receipt on exactly that status: "if (!vorgang || vorgang.status !== 'abgeschlossen') return;" (line 188), so getValueReceipt is never called, and the receipt / once_only / source_line / ValueReceiptCard blocks (lines 282-305) never render. The spine e2e tacitly confirms this — it asserts only 4 confirmed Block-A cards and notes "The 5th card (first Block-D step) may remain in_progress/pending" (spine.spec.ts:280-286); it never asserts the receipt or once_only counter.
findings.explanation: wow-backlog #1's entire thesis is "Erleichterung mit Beweis" — the citizen says one sentence, watches, and is handed a receipt counting what they did NOT have to do ("ca. 38 Felder", Once-Only). For the default demo persona that climactic receipt and counter silently never appear, because two eID-gated steps hang. The Loom's emotional climax and the demo-spine's headline payoff are structurally unreachable for Anna. A skeptical reviewer who runs the repo (not just watches the cut Loom) sees a cascade that stalls with two perpetual spinners and no receipt — directly undermining the trust claim that the system finishes the job and accounts for itself.
findings.recommendation: Decide the Block-D model and make it real: either (a) wire the eID-confirm button so the citizen completes Block D and the vorgang reaches abgeschlossen, or (b) treat eID-pending as a terminal 'awaiting you' state that isVorgangFullyResolved tolerates so the receipt renders with those steps shown as "Ihre Bestätigung ausstehend". Add a spine e2e assertion on the ValueReceiptCard + once_only line so this regression is caught.

verdict.reasoning: Every link in the critique's chain verified in code. (1) isVorgangFullyResolved (api.ts:576-583) requires EVERY step ∈ {confirmed,failed,self_assigned} — verbatim. (2) Anna's persona (src/data/personas.json) has kindergeld_bezug:true and aufenthaltstitel:present (§18g AufenthG) → the live cascade fires two Block-D steps: familienkasse-berlin-brandenburg + abh-berlin-lea (kfz_halter is false, so KFZ does NOT fire — the EVIDENCE's incidental KFZ mention is wrong, but the two steps it actually names are correct). (3) umzugAutopilot (src/lib/mock-backend/autopilot/umzug.ts:614-634) yields Block-D steps at status 'pending_eid_confirmation' and does NOT wait; advancing them requires the UI to call api.bestätigeAutopilotSchritt. (4) DECISIVE: a full-repo grep shows bestätigeAutopilotSchritt/bestaetigeAutopilotSchritt is never invoked by ANY .tsx component — not InlineCascade, not the run page. No confirm button, no onClick, no auto-confirm timer; all component references to pending_eid_confirmation are display-only icon/label maps. So both Block-D steps hang forever → isVorgangFullyResolved is permanently false → changeVorgangStatus(...,'abgeschlossen') at api.ts:1463/1493 is never reached. (5) InlineCascade.tsx:188 gates getValueReceipt on vorgang.status==='abgeschlossen', so the receipt + once_only line (282-286) + source_line (288-292) + ValueReceiptCard (303-305) never render for Anna. findings.title: SSoT Stammdaten is framed responsibly as a converged VIEW, but the demo never visually demonstrates that it is NOT a new central super-register — the strongest German privacy objection goes unanswered on-screen
findings.evidence: de.json copy is careful in prose: stammdaten.hero.vision_2027_banner_body = "...wie eine konvergierte Bürger:innen-Sicht auf das hypothetische 2027-Bild aus BundID-Stammdatenservice, Datenschutzcockpit nach IDNrG und EUDI-Wallet-PID aussehen könnte", and the Datenschutz-Cockpit register entries are register-to-register (UebermittlungsLogEntry kategorie 'behoerde_zu_behoerde', api.ts:1440) per the wow-backlog #13 correction. But this anti-super-register framing lives in a banner sentence and a cockpit log; nothing in the spine/Loom (Szene 7 shows the cockpit for 1-2s) actively shows that data stays in source registers and the app only holds a view. The Volkszählungsurteil-shaped objection ('this is the centralized Personenkennzeichen / super-register we rejected in 1983') is the predictable reflex of the target audience and is answered only in fine print.
findings.explanation: Informationelle Selbstbestimmung makes 'one central profile of the citizen' the most loaded concept in German GovTech. The project clearly KNOWS this (the converged-view wording, the register-to-register cockpit, the IDNrG references). But a 3-minute Loom and the dashboard never make the decentralization legible as a felt point — the cockpit gets a 1-2s pan. A Datenschützer's first question ('where does this data live, who is the controller, is this a new register?') is technically answerable from the codebase but is not surfaced in the demo's narrative beats, so the objection stands unrebutted for the viewer who matters.
findings.recommendation: Add one explicit on-screen/Loom beat that names the architecture: 'Kein neues Zentralregister — die Daten bleiben in den Quellregistern; diese Ansicht ist nur eine Sicht, Sie sind Herr Ihrer Daten (informationelle Selbstbestimmung), jede Übermittlung steht im Datenschutzcockpit.' Promote the cockpit from a 1-2s pan to a deliberate beat that shows a register-to-register transmission line.

verdict.reasoning:
I tried to refute this and could not on the merits, but the claimed severity is overstated.

GROUNDED IN DOMAIN REALITY (verified via web search): The Volkszählungsurteil / "no central Personenkennzeichen / Superregister" objection is real, live, and the predictable first reflex of exactly this audience (DigitalService, BMDS, GovTech Deutschland). There is an active Verfassungsbeschwerde against the RegMoG; the Bundesdatenschutzbeauftragte called a unified personal ID unconstitutional; netzpolitik framing is "Eine Nummer für alles und jeden / gläserne Bürger." Crucially, the GOVERNMENT'S OWN canonical rebuttal is literally the message the auditor recommends: digitale-verwaltung.de / BMI / Bremen state "Es wird kein neuer zentraler Datenbestand geschaffen ... die Informationen verbleiben unverändert in den dezentralen Registern," with the Datenschutzcockpit as the transparency mechanism. So the recommendation is the established correct counter, not a strawman the demo doesn't claim.

GROUNDED IN THE ARTIFACTS: The auditor's evidence checks out. de.json stammdaten.hero.vision_2027_banner_body is exactly the careful converged-view prose quoted (line 1466). The cascade log model is genuinely register-to-register (api.ts:1437-1448, kategorie 'behoerde_zu_behoerde', absender buergeramt-berlin-mitte → empfaenger step.behoerde_id), matching the wow-backlog #13 correction. The STRONGEST explicit anti-register statement (disclaimer.lese_schicht line 1779: "Diese App ... nimmt KEINE Änderungen an Ihren Daten in den zuständigen Registern vor ... Korrekturen ausschließlich im jeweiligen Behörden-Verfahren") is real but is rendered inside a COLLAPSED <details> in StammdatenHero.tsx:176 — i.e. genuinely fine print. The Loom (loom-script.md Szene 7) gives the cockpit only a ~1-2s pan, and the spine table never foregrounds decentralization. The planned wow-#1 "Stammdaten-Quellzeile" is a Once-Only source line ("Quelle: Ihre Stammdaten, einmal bestätigt am…"), NOT a "no central register" beat, and #13 (the register-to-register feed) is explicitly deprioritized as not advancing the hero. So "the strongest objection is answered only in fine print, never as a felt narrative beat" is factually accurate.

WHY SEVERITY IS LOWER THAN CLAIMED (medium → low): This is an emphasis/copy gap on top of an already-correct architecture, copy, and data model — not a missing concept or a self-contradiction. The headline wow ("one sentence → six authorities each notified") is itself a decentralized, register-to-register story; the demo never asserts the super-register it would be accused of. The affected surfaces (Stammdaten, Datenschutz-Cockpit) are explicitly the "supporting/frozen" track, not the spine. The fix is one Loom beat + one promoted line of already-written copy — trivial, and aligned with the project's own #13 correction. Real but bounded: it forfeits a rebuttal opportunity with one important segment, it does not break credibility. Specific, substantiated, actionable — confirmed, at low strategic impact.

Sources: bundestag.de RegMoG; humanistische-union.de Verfassungsbeschwerde; netzpolitik.org; digitale-verwaltung.de Datenschutzcockpit; bmi.bund.de RegMoG FAQ; finanzen.bremen.de DSC.

findings.title: Headline wow contradicts your own domain doc: the Loom counts Rundfunkbeitrag + Arbeitgeber as 'Behörden' — the exact tell an insider catches in 30 seconds
findings.evidence: Loom Szene 0/5/Cold-Open say verbatim: "Einwohnermeldeamt, Finanzamt, KFZ-Stelle, Krankenkasse, Rundfunkbeitrag, Arbeitgeber … jede zuständige Behörde für Sie … Sechs Meldungen, ein Satz." But your OWN wow-backlog.md:21 states the non-negotiable rule: "Beitragsservice (Anstalt) + Arbeitgeber (privat) sind KEINE Behörden und zählen nicht in einen 'X Behörden'-Count." The code already honours this — value-receipt.ts:32 PRIVATE_ODER_ANSTALT excludes beitragsservice-koeln + arbeitgeber-mittelstand-software from behoerden_count, and de.json:2948 renders the careful '{count} Behörden'. The spoken headline does NOT.
findings.explanation: Your primary viewers (DigitalService, BMDS) live inside the Föderalismus/Trägerschaft distinctions daily. 'Der Beitragsservice ist eine Anstalt, der Arbeitgeber eine Privatstelle, das sind keine Behörden' is reflex knowledge. The spoken claim 'sechs Behörden / jede zuständige Behörde' is the first thing the reviewer hears, and it is factually wrong by your own filter. It signals 'consumer-pitch gloss bolted onto careful internals' — the worst impression for this audience, because it makes them distrust everything they cannot personally verify. The careful code becomes invisible; the wrong sentence is what lands.
findings.recommendation: Re-script the headline to 'sechs Stellen' or 'sechs Empfänger' (which is true and still punchy), and explicitly name the split on screen — '4 Behörden automatisch (BMG), Beitragsservice (Anstalt), Arbeitgeber (mit Einwilligung)'. The honesty IS the differentiation for this audience: showing you know Beitragsservice is not a Behörde is a stronger signal than the round number. Align loom-script.md and demo-spine.md:11 to the rule already enforced in value-receipt.ts.

verdict.reasoning:
GROUNDED and accurate. I verified all four legs of the claim against the actual files and against German administrative law.

(1) The Loom narration mislabels non-Behörden as Behörden — verbatim. loom-script.md:83 (Szene 5): "Eine Behörde nach der anderen empfängt die Adressänderung: Einwohnermeldeamt, Finanzamt, KFZ-Stelle, Krankenkasse, Rundfunkbeitrag, Arbeitgeber." loom-script.md:125 (Cold-Open): "informiert das System jede zuständige Behörde für Sie — Meldeamt, Finanzamt, KFZ-Stelle, Krankenkasse, Rundfunk, Arbeitgeber." demo-spine.md:11 (the canonical Headline wow): "notifies every competent Behörde for them — Einwohnermeldeamt, Finanzamt, KFZ-Stelle, Krankenkasse, Rundfunkbeitrag, Arbeitgeber." All three call Rundfunkbeitrag (Beitragsservice) and Arbeitgeber "Behörde / zuständige Behörde."

(2) This contradicts the project's OWN non-negotiable rule. wow-backlog.md:21 Hard Rule: "Beitragsservice (Anstalt) + Arbeitgeber (privat) sind KEINE Behörden und zählen nicht in einen 'X Behörden'-Count." wow-backlog.md:50 flag (d) repeats it for this very feature.

(3) The code already honours the rule. value-receipt.ts:32-40 defines PRIVATE_ODER_ANSTALT excluding 'arbeitgeber-mittelstand-software' and 'beitragsservice-koeln'; lines 60-65/82 compute behoerden_count over distinct PUBLIC Behörden only. Its own comment (line 8) pegs "Anna live: 6 Behoerden" AFTER excluding Beitragsservice/Arbeitgeber/Bundesdruckerei — so the model's honest "six" is a DIFFERENT set of six than the Loom's enumerated six. The UI strings are likewise honest: de.json:2914 says "zuständige Stellen" (not Behörden) on the completion banner, de.json:652 "Beteiligte Stellen ({count})", and the count render de.json:2948 is plural-driven "{# Behörden}". So the on-screen product gets it right; only the spoken narration + headline doc do not.

(4) The German-law premise is correct. WebSearch confirms the Beitragsservice is explicitly "neither an authority" — a public-law Verwaltungsgemeinschaft/GbR without legal capacity (Wikipedia, Bundestag WD). Under §1 Abs.4 VwVfG a private employer (Privatperson) is not a Behörde unless beliehen. So labelling Rundfunkbeitrag + Arbeitgeber as "Behörden" is factually wrong, and the audience (DigitalService/BMDS) would catch it.

Minor inaccuracy that does not refute: the auditor compresses the spoken line to "sechs Behörden"; the screens actually say "Sechs Meldungen, ein Satz" (correct word) and "eine Behörde nach der anderen … [non-Behörden]" / "jede zuständige Behörde für Sie — [non-Behörden]". The substantive error — applying the label "Behörde" to Rundfunkbeitrag + Arbeitgeber — is real and verbatim. Also Szene 0 (line 19) does use the correct word "Stellen", showing the script is internally inconsistent rather than uniformly wrong.

Severity corrected critical→high: it is a genuine, specific, audience-salient credibility tell that violates the team's own Hard Rule and is the first thing the target reviewer hears — but it does NOT break the demo, the on-screen product is already honest ("Stellen"), and the fix is a two-file re-script (use "Stellen/Empfänger", name the Behörde/Anstalt/Privatstelle split). The recommendation is correct and already half-implemented in the UI strings. That is high strategic impact, not critical.

findings.title: The README/PRD never engages with what DigitalService actually ships today — no prior-art positioning
findings.location: docs/PRD.md (whole doc still 'draft — to be filled in'; no reference to existing DE services)

verdict.reasoning:
PARTIALLY GROUNDED, but the framing is a strawman and the severity is inflated.

WHAT IS TRUE (verified):
- PRD.md:3 is literally still "Status: draft — to be filled in"; it names target orgs (PRD.md:13) and never references a single shipped product of those orgs. Accurate.
- The README (the primary portfolio-facing artifact) has NO status-quo/prior-art positioning. Grep over README.md returns zero hits for BundID, Verwaltungsportal, OZG, RegMoG, gov.uk, Estonia, "Inspirierende Vorbilder", or any "Verhältnis zum Status quo" beat. It pitches AT DigitalService/BMDS/Tech4Germany/GovTech/GovStart (README:156) without situating itself against what they run. The Loom script (docs/loom-script.md) likewise has no prior-art beat.
- The factual premise is sound. WebSearch confirms BundID is real and as of Jan 2026 near-mandatory, the Verwaltungsportal-Verbund/Bundesportal exists, OZG/EfA reuse is famously weak (~14/306 EfA services reused; <half of 575 bundles online). antragsloses Kindergeld as real 2027 law is corroborated in the project's own dated research. So the ecosystem the candidate pitches at is exactly as the auditor describes.
- So the NARROW, ACTIONABLE recommendation — add a short "Verhältnis zum Status quo" section to README/PRD (BundID, Verwaltungsportal-Verbund, OZG/RegMoG, antragsloses Kindergeld) — is valid, correctly located, and cheap.

WHERE THE CRITIQUE OVERREACHES (refuted):
- The headline ("never engages with what DigitalService actually ships," "domain docs ... silent on the institutional landscape," "reads as an outsider's mock-up / naive outsider") is factually false at the PROJECT level. docs/research/public-repos-landscape.md catalogs DigitalService's ACTUAL portfolio by name — elterngeldrechner, a2j-rechtsantragstelle, and grundsteuer (the exact "tax/Grundsteuer tooling" the EVIDENCE claims is missing) — plus KERN, KoliBri (ITZBund), the EUDI reference impl, and Tech4Germany cohort repos, with licenses and last-activity dates. docs/research/2026-05-30-prior-art-it-did-it-for-me.md engages OZG's ~15%-digital figure, NOOTS/Registermodernisierung, ELSTER VaSt, antragsloses Kindergeld (dated Kabinettsbeschluss), and gov.uk Tell Us Once / Estonia Data Tracker as benchmarks. docs/domain/umzug.md cites the governing BMG/FZV statutes. The institutional knowledge is present and unusually rigorous — this is a PACKAGING/surfacing gap, not a knowledge gap. The research file even carries an explicit unshipped action item: "Cite both in our README under 'Inspirierende Vorbilder'." So the omission is known and planned, not a blind spot.

NET: a real, low-cost presentation gap in the portfolio-facing artifacts (confirmed), wrapped in a "naive outsider" characterization that the project's own docs refute (refuted). Strategic impact is modest, not the credibility-collapse the explanation implies — hence correctedSeverity = low, verdict = partial.

findings.title: Autopilot-cascade is the right emotional wow but the wrong CREDIBILITY wow for procurement/legal-bound insiders — the consent toggle reads as the naive part
findings.location: concept (docs/specs/umzug.md Block A/B/C/D model; loom-script.md:71-83 Szene 4/5)
findings.evidence: The legal gating is genuinely thoughtful — Block A auto (§§33/34/36 BMG), Block B consent (Art. 6(1)(a) DSGVO, widerruflich), Block C self-do, Block D eID-confirmed (per the brief and umzug.md:35). But the Loom dramatises the CONFIRM-AND-WATCH moment (loom-script.md:83 'jetzt arbeitet das System für mich … eine Behörde nach der anderen empfängt'), not the governance moment. A consumer sees magic; a Datenschutz/procurement insider sees an unanswered question: who is the Verantwortlicher for this cross-Behörden orchestration, on what Rechtsgrundlage does a single private operator route data between a Land-Finanzamt and a kommunale KFZ-Stelle, and how does that survive a DSFA / the Zweckbindung?
findings.explanation: For this audience the wow is not 'six things happened fast' — they have sat through the Datenschutz-Folgenabschätzung that kills exactly this orchestration. The cascade impresses the citizen-viewer (correctly, for the Loom's stated 70% engagement metric) but to a BfDI-adjacent or procurement reviewer the un-foregrounded question 'wer ist hier die verantwortliche Stelle, und ist das überhaupt zulässig' makes the speed feel naive rather than visionary. The strength (it looks effortless) is the weakness (effortless is what the legal reality forbids).
findings.recommendation: Foreground governance as a deliberate beat, not a disclaimer afterthought. GROUNDED on both axes, so I cannot refute it — but it overstates the gap, hence partial not confirmed.

Severity: keep MEDIUM but leaning low — a single missing sentence on a demo that already does more governance work than the critique credits, cushioned by the speculative frame; offset upward because the fix is cheap, accurate, and high-leverage for the precise NOOTS-literate audience that opens the doors this portfolio targets.

findings.title: Value receipt, Once-Only counter and Stammdaten source line NEVER render inline for the demo persona
findings.evidence: InlineCascade only fetches the receipt when `vorgang.status === 'abgeschlossen'` (line 188). The receipt-dependent UI is all gated on `receipt`: Once-Only counter (line 282-286), Stammdaten source line (line 288-292), and `<ValueReceiptCard>` (line 303-305). The vorgang only flips to `abgeschlossen` when `isVorgangFullyResolved` is true, which requires EVERY step to be `confirmed|failed|self_assigned` (api.ts:576-582). findings.explanation: Four of the five 'folded candidates' that justified this whole feature (consequence receipt finale, Once-Only counter, Stammdaten source line, value-receipt count-up) are wired so they are invisible for the exact persona the Loom uses (Anna). The wow-backlog's promise — 'eine Quittung am Ende, die zählt, was Sie NICHT tun mussten' (#1 Bürger-Pitch) — does not fire in the demo. The emotional climax ('hier ist der Beleg') is structurally unreachable inline. The feature ships its scaffolding but not its punchline.
findings.recommendation: Decouple the receipt from full `abgeschlossen` resolution OR ensure Block-D auto-confirms in-session (see prior finding). Concretely: fetch/show the value receipt once all NON-eID steps (Block A + consented Block B) are confirmed, treating pending-eID steps as a separate 'you confirm these' tail. Add an e2e assertion that the inline ValueReceiptCard + Once-Only counter become visible after the run, so this regression can't silently persist.

verdict.reasoning:
Every code claim verified against source. The generator emits both as status 'pending_eid_confirmation' (umzug.ts:626) and does NOT wait. 'pending_eid_confirmation' is in neither the resolved set, so after runAutopilotInBackground finishes (api.ts:1492-1497) the vorgang stays non-abgeschlossen → getValueReceipt never fires inline → none of the receipt UI renders for Anna. The spine e2e (246-252) asserts only the testid + a Behörden row + the kept link, and 282-286 explicitly tolerates the 5th/Block-D card not resolving — corroborating that the inline receipt is never expected to appear. So the four 'folded candidates' (receipt finale, Once-Only counter, Stammdaten source line, count-up) are invisible for the exact demo persona. The wow-1 spec's pitch (line 12: 'mit einer Quittung, die zählt') and §6.4/§11 ('Block-A completes → status abgeschlossen → receipt arrives') are contradicted by isVorgangFullyResolved, which the spec flagged as an unverified TODO.

AGGRAVATING: this is not inline-only. run/page.tsx:214 uses the identical abgeschlossen gate, and there is NO eID-confirm affordance anywhere — EidConfirmDialog has zero importers and bestätigeAutopilotSchritt/bestaetigeAutopilotSchritt is called by no component (grep confirms only the API definition + the umzug.ts comment). (2) Slot 5 (Familienkasse) is a Block-D step that sits at pending_eid_confirmation and only flips to confirmed after a manual bestätigeAutopilotSchritt eID click (umzug.ts:614-634) — in an auto-run it stays a spinner, exactly the "one of which is a stalled spinner" point. findings.title: The demo opens with German-admin-pain stakes that a non-German / cold viewer cannot feel
findings.evidence: Scene 0 opens cold with 'Ein Umzug in Deutschland ist kein Vorgang — es sind sechs. Einwohnermeldeamt, Finanzamt, KFZ-Stelle, Krankenkasse, Rundfunkbeitrag, Arbeitgeber.' Scene 2 then dwells 25s on Dashboard internals ('Aufenthaltstitel, Steuerbescheid, Stromzähler', status-badges). The first concrete demonstration of the product's value (the cascade) does not begin until ~1:50 (Scene 5 timing [1:50–2:25]).
findings.explanation: The headline claim ('sechs Stellen für ein Lebensereignis') is asserted, not shown — a viewer who hasn't personally suffered the German Behördengang has no felt baseline for why six notifications is painful, so the later relief has nothing to land against. Spending the first ~110 seconds on login + a dashboard tour before the payoff is a classic attention cliff: portfolio reviewers and fellowship screeners watch the first 30-60s. The strongest asset (cascade) is buried past the point most cold viewers disengage.
findings.recommendation: Lead with the Cold-Open universally (not as an optional variant), and make the stakes CONCRETE and visual in the first 10s: show the six separate forms/queues/letters the citizen would otherwise face (a quick montage or a single 'today: 6 separate processes' frame), THEN 'say it once'. Move the cascade payoff before 0:60. Cut the Scene-2 dashboard tour to a 5s glance at the Umzug nudge only.

verdict.reasoning:
The critique's central recommendation is already implemented in the very file it cites. docs/loom-script.md:116-130 contains a "Cold-Open-Variante (20-30s) — führt mit dem Wow" that opens with "Stellen Sie sich vor, Sie ziehen um — und sagen das genau einmal," immediately types the trigger, confirms, and runs the cascade — payoff under ~0:30. The header (line 12) and director notes (lines 137-140) flag it explicitly for "aufmerksamkeitsknappe Zuschauer." The auditor's "cascade buried past 1:50 / first 110s on login+dashboard" describes ONLY the 3-minute long-form variant and ignores the cold-open sitting 90 lines below the lines they cited (16-24, 40-48). So the auditor's recommendation ("lead with cold-open, move payoff before 0:60") collapses to a defensible editorial preference — make cold-open the *default* vs. keep it a *labeled variant* — not a high-severity defect.

Three further weaknesses in the critique: (1) Its empirical anchor for "high" severity — "portfolio reviewers and fellowship screeners watch the first 30-60s" — is asserted, not established. Web research confirms a real "opening cliff" exists, but that body of evidence is YouTube/algorithmic-distribution behavior for casual browsers, not submitted-Loom review by a motivated fellowship screener (a captive, intentional viewer). The principle has merit; the specific claim is overstated. (2) The recommended fix — a "six separate forms/queues/letters" stakes montage — collides with a hard project guardrail the brief and wow-backlog enforce repeatedly: Beitragsservice and Arbeitgeber are NOT Behörden and several recipients aren't auto-pushed today; a literal "six Behörden queues" montage risks the exact overclaim the project polices, so the recommendation is not cost-free. (3) The critique attacks a moving target: branch feat/wow-1-inline-cascade (demo-spine.md:44, wow-backlog #1) exists specifically to make the cascade continuous and inline in the assistant thread — removing the Scene-5 "Kaskade ansehen" link-away and moving the payoff materially earlier — and the Loom will be re-cut against it. findings.title: Wow-backlog sells 'ca. 38 Felder' but the implemented Once-Only counter shows ~9 — narrative/number mismatch
findings.evidence: wow-backlog.md:42 pitches the counter as 'ca. 38 Felder vorausgefüllt'. The implemented derivation sums `step.datenkategorien.length` over confirmed steps (value-receipt.ts:72-77). The spec's own worked example: 'Bürgeramt=3 · Finanzamt=2 · Beitragsservice=3 · Bundesdruckerei=1 → once_only_fields = 9 for Block-A-only' (wow-1-inline-cascade.md:168). Since Block D never confirms inline for Anna (see critical findings) and Block B is consent-dependent, the inline number for the demo is in the ~9-15 range, not 38.
findings.explanation: This is the GOOD direction on honesty (flag (b) 'must be derived, not invented' was respected). But it creates a presentation gap: the punchy number that motivated the feature ('38') is roughly 4x the number that actually appears, and in many demo runs the counter won't render at all (gated on receipt). 'ca. 9 Felder' is a far weaker rhetorical hit than the team planned for, and if any pitch deck / README / Loom voiceover quotes 38 while the screen shows 9, that is the first place a numerate reviewer says 'belegen Sie das'.
findings.recommendation: Pick one honest, defensible number and make it appear consistently: ensure the receipt renders (fix the abgeschlossen gating) so the counter shows, and align all narrative copy (README, Loom, any deck) to the actual derived value rather than the 38 placeholder. If a bigger number is wanted, count per-recipient field transmissions across all confirmed hops (already the design) AND make the consented Block B + Block D confirm so the sum legitimately grows — never restore the invented 38.

verdict.reasoning:
The auditor's ARITHMETIC is correct but the framed WEAKNESS (a damaging demo-narrative/wow presentation gap) is not grounded in the project's actual external claims.

WHAT'S TRUE: computeValueReceipt (src/lib/mock-backend/value-receipt.ts:72-77) sums step.datenkategorien.length over confirmed steps. For Anna's inline demo run only Block A confirms inline (Block D yields status 'pending_eid_confirmation' and the generator does NOT await it — umzug.ts:613-634; it confirms only via a separate bestätigeAutopilotSchritt call). Block A = Bürgeramt[3] + Finanzamt[2] + Beitragsservice[3] + Bundesdruckerei[1] = 9. The spec's own worked example (wow-1-inline-cascade.md:168) states exactly this: "once_only_fields = 9 for Block-A-only ... UI renders 'ca. 9'." So the rendered number is ~9, not 38. Anna persona flags confirm this: kfz_halter:false (no KFZ), kindergeld_bezug:true + aufenthaltstitel present (Familienkasse/ABH are Block D, eID-gated, don't confirm inline).

WHY THE WEAKNESS IS REFUTED: (1) "38" never reaches ANY user-facing artefact. I grepped the whole repo (excl. node_modules): "38 Felder" appears in exactly THREE internal planning docs — wow-backlog.md:42 (ranked brainstorm), 2026-05-30-citizen-pain-relief-moments.md:33 (research doc, explicitly an illustrative example), and wow-1-inline-cascade.md:168 (the spec — where it actually computes 9; 38 appears nowhere as a target). The Loom script (docs/loom-script.md), README.md, PRD.md, and demo-spine.md quote NO field count at all. The Loom's only headline number is "Sechs Meldungen, ein Satz." The implemented i18n label is "Felder, die Sie nicht ausfüllen mussten" with a ca.-prefixed derived N. (2) The team already caught and resolved this: the wow-backlog HARD FLAG (b) sitting next to "38" mandates the count "MUSS ... ABGELEITET sein, nicht erfunden ... keine Scheingenauigkeit"; the spec (C6, §5.2, §11/R1) and the value-receipt header comment ("KEINE erfundenen Werte") encode it; the implementation derives it. 38 was a brainstorm placeholder the pipeline explicitly flagged as must-be-derived — the auditor even concedes "flag (b) was respected." (3) The auditor's harm scenario is explicitly CONDITIONAL ("if any pitch deck / README / Loom voiceover quotes 38") and that antecedent is FALSE in the repo. The predicted "belegen Sie das" moment cannot arise from this source.

SECONDARY CLAIM PARTLY WRONG: the auditor calls the abgeschlossen gate a bug to "fix." For the spine demo (Anna, ?reliable=1) Block A completes → status 'abgeschlossen' → receipt renders (spec §6.4). The null-until-confirmed behavior is documented B1 design, not a defect.

RESIDUAL VALID KERNEL: a stale "38" string sits in two internal docs and could in principle be copied into a future deck by someone who reads the backlog instead of the binding spec. That is doc-hygiene (delete/annotate the placeholder), not a medium demo-narrative defect — the rendered "ca. 9" is honest, derived, and is the better anti-overclaim outcome the project's own guardrails demanded. True impact = low, trending to none for the demo itself; claimed "medium" overstates it.

findings.title: Redundant 'Kaskade ansehen' link beside the inline cascade dilutes the single-beat the feature exists to create
findings.evidence: ToolCallCard renders `<InlineCascade>` immediately followed by a still-present `<Link href={/vorgaenge/umzug/run...}>{t('cta_kaskade')}</Link>` (lines 93-100). InlineCascade also renders its OWN '/posteingang' link (InlineCascade.tsx:294-299). The spine e2e even guards the link's continued existence ('the kept "Kaskade ansehen" link still co-exists inline', spine.spec.ts:251-252).
findings.explanation: The entire thesis of wow-1 was to stop the click-away so 'the magic stays in the thread the user is watching' (wow-backlog.md:38). Keeping a prominent 'Kaskade ansehen →' link right under the inline cascade re-invites the click-away the feature was built to eliminate, and during a live demo it tempts the presenter to navigate off (which the Loom script unfortunately still instructs). Two competing CTAs (run page + posteingang) plus the inline view is a muddled call-to-action at the climax.
findings.recommendation: Demote the 'Kaskade ansehen' link to a low-emphasis 'Details ansehen' shown only AFTER the inline run completes (or remove it for the live variant), so the inline cascade + its posteingang link are the single forward path. Keep the /run route reachable from the Vorgang detail, not as a peer CTA at the wow moment.

verdict.reasoning:
CODE EVIDENCE CONFIRMED, but the core thesis is REFUTED by a documented design decision; only a narrow doc-sync sub-point survives.

What I verified in code:
- ToolCallCard.tsx:91-102 does render <InlineCascade> immediately followed by a <Link href="/vorgaenge/umzug/run?...">{t('cta_kaskade')}</Link> ("Kaskade ansehen"). Confirmed.
- InlineCascade.tsx:294-299 also renders its own /posteingang link. Confirmed.
- spine.spec.ts:251-252 guards the kept link's continued existence. Confirmed.

Why the central critique ("the kept link dilutes the single beat / re-invites the click-away / is a muddled peer CTA") is REFUTED:
1. The kept link is an EXPLICIT, USER-LOCKED design decision, not an oversight. docs/specs/wow-1-inline-cascade.md §1 reads verbatim: "Decided (locked by the user — do not relitigate): 1. Inline hero + a quiet kept link. The cascade plays INLINE ... A secondary link to /vorgaenge/umzug/run stays for the detailed/historic view." C1/§7/§12 reinforce: the inline cascade is "additive," rendered ABOVE the link, and the link must not be renamed/removed. The auditor frames a deliberate choice as a dilution bug.
2. It is NOT a "prominent peer CTA." In code the link is a low-emphasis inline text link (text-sm font-medium text-primary, self-start — not a filled button), rendered BELOW the inline hero (live Behörden rows + ValueReceiptCard + Once-Only counter + Stammdaten source line). The inline beat is the visual hero; the two text links are subordinate. The spec's "quiet kept link" intent matches the implementation.
3. The "two competing CTAs" framing is misleading: the /posteingang link is the spec's INTENDED forward path (spine step 6 = "confirmations land in Posteingang"), and /run is a secondary detail/historic view. They are not symmetric rival CTAs.
4. No German-admin/legal factual assertion is made by this item (it is a pure UX/narrative judgment), so there is nothing legal to disconfirm — and the project's stated scope explicitly sanctions the link, so the critique attacks something the demo deliberately claims.

The ONE grounded residual (why partial, not fully refuted): the Loom script is stale relative to the inline-cascade re-wire. docs/loom-script.md Szene 5 (lines 86-88) still scripts clicking "Kaskade ansehen →" to the /run timeline and calls THAT "der Wow-Moment," and Regie-Notiz line 136 says "Der Wow gehört Szene 5." That does undercut the new inline beat for the actual recording — the auditor's observation that "the Loom script unfortunately still instructs" the navigation is literally correct. But this is a documentation-sync gap in loom-script.md, NOT the code weakness at the cited LOCATION (ToolCallCard.tsx:91-102), which behaves exactly as the locked spec requires. Strategic impact is low: the link is quiet and intentional; the only real fix is updating the Loom script so the demo lingers on the inline beat before (optionally) showing /run as a detail view. Recommendation to "demote/remove the link" overreaches against an explicit user lock; the actionable, in-scope fix is the Loom script, not the component.
